The effective potential matrixes of the J=1/2𝐽12J=1/2 (Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs, Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD), (Λc​D¯s∗subscriptΛ𝑐superscriptsubscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs*, Ξc​D¯∗subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD*) systems, and the J=3/2𝐽32J=3/2 (Λc​D¯s∗subscriptΛ𝑐superscriptsubscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs*, Ξc​D¯∗subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD*) system share the same expressions in the heavy quark limit. From Table , we obtain the corresponding effective potential matrix 𝕍=(v11v12v21v22)𝕍subscript𝑣11subscript𝑣12subscript𝑣21subscript𝑣22displaystyleV=left(beginarrayccv11v12 v21v22 endarrayright) with v11subscript𝑣11displaystyle v11 =displaystyle= −43​gs,v22=−103​gs,43subscript𝑔𝑠subscript𝑣22103subscript𝑔𝑠displaystyle-frac43tildegs,quad v22=-frac103tildegs, v12subscript𝑣12displaystyle v12 =displaystyle= v21=2​2​gs​gx. subscript𝑣2122subscript𝑔𝑠subscript𝑔𝑥displaystyle v21=2sqrt2tildegsgx. Here, for the diagonal matrix elements listed in Table and , their dominant components are from the exchange of the non-strange light scalar meson currents. Since the interactions of the off-diagonal channel Λc​D¯s(∗)−Ξc​D¯(∗)subscriptΛ𝑐subscriptsuperscript¯𝐷𝑠subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷LambdacbarD(*)s-XicbarD(*) are introduced via the exchange of the strange scalar meson currents, we further introduce a factor gxsubscript𝑔𝑥gx to estimate the SU breaking effects. Compared with the exchange of the non-strange light scalar meson currents, the off-diagonal matrix elements should be suppressed by the mass of strange mesons. Thus, we assume 0≤g​x≤10𝑔𝑥10leq gxleq 1. This factor also reflects the coupling strength of the Λc​D¯s−Ξc​D¯subscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷LambdacbarDs-XicbarD channel. With gx=0subscript𝑔𝑥0gx=0, the Λc​D¯s(∗)subscriptΛ𝑐subscriptsuperscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarD(*)s does not couple to the Ξc​D¯(∗)subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD(*) channel. With gx=1.0subscript𝑔𝑥1.0gx=1.0, the Λc​D¯s(∗)subscriptΛ𝑐subscriptsuperscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarD(*)s couples to the Ξc​D¯(∗)subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD(*) channel and its coupling strength is set to be the value in the SU limit. In Fig. (b), we present the variation of the masses for the bound states Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs and Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs as the parameter gxsubscript𝑔𝑥gx increases. The masses of the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs and Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs are denoted with black lines. At gx=0subscript𝑔𝑥0gx=0, the Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs channel itself has a weak attractive force v11=−4/3​gssubscript𝑣1143subscript𝑔𝑠v11=-4/3tildegs, and this force is too weak to form a Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs bound state. On the contrary, the Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD channel can form a bound state and its mass is about M=4329𝑀4329M=4329 MeV, slightly smaller than the experimental value. As the gxsubscript𝑔𝑥gx increases, the attractive force of the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs decreases and its mass moves closer to the Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD threshold. In a very narrow region 0.62≤gx≤0.640.62subscript𝑔𝑥0.640.62leq gxleq 0.64, the attractive force is just enough to form a Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs bound state at the Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD threshold and the weak attractive channel Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs starts to form a bound state due to the Ξc​D¯−Λc​D¯ssubscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷subscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠XicbarD-LambdacbarDs coupling. Only in this very narrow region, the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs and Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs can coexist as quasi-bound states. At gx>0.64subscript𝑔𝑥0.64gx>0.64, the Ξc​D¯−Λc​D¯ssubscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷subscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠XicbarD-LambdacbarDs coupling further weakens the attractive force of the Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD channel and the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs no longer exists as a quasi-bound state, while the attractive force of the Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs channel becomes stronger and its mass will decrease. The observation of the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs by LHCb seems to exclude the parameter region 0.640.64subscript𝑔𝑥0.64gx>0.64 in the energy region slightly above the Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD threshold. We find that the pole of the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs still exists in the first Riemann sheet. This is mainly due to the fact that the Λc​D¯s−Ξc​D¯subscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷LambdacbarDs-XicbarD coupling leads the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs to be a state that have a considerable width, thus the central value of the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs mass may cross the Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD threshold. In this case, the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs should be interpreted as a quasi-bound state above the Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD threshold. Nevertheless, in this work, we restrict our scope to the case that the masses of the bound/quasi-bound states are below their corresponding thresholds. To understand why the gxsubscript𝑔𝑥gx region that allows the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs and Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs states to coexist is so narrow, we further check the role of the Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs channel in our two-channel model. We allow the effective potential of the Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs channel to have a 20%percent2020% shift, i. e. , v11′=v11+v11δ,v11δ=0,±15​v11,formulae-sequencesubscriptsuperscript𝑣′11subscript𝑣11subscriptsuperscript𝑣𝛿11subscriptsuperscript𝑣𝛿110plus-or-minus15subscript𝑣11displaystyle vprime11=v11+vdelta11,quad vdelta11=0,pmfrac15v11, and further check how the masses of the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs and Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs change as we increase the gxsubscript𝑔𝑥gx. The channel Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs itself has a weak attractive force, as presented in Fig. (a), at gx=0subscript𝑔𝑥0gx=0. After we increase this force by 20%percent%, this single-channel still can not form a bound state. But the gxsubscript𝑔𝑥gx region that allows these two Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states to coexist becomes broader. On the contrary, as illustrated in Fig. (c), if we decrease the attractive force of the Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs channel by 20%percent%, the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs and Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs can not coexist no matter how we adjust the off-diagonal Λc​D¯s−Ξc​D¯subscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷LambdacbarDs-XicbarD coupling. Thus, the narrow gxsubscript𝑔𝑥gx region that the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs and Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs can coexist is due to the fact that the Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs channel has a small but non-negligible attractive force. The results for the J=1/2𝐽12J=1/2 and 3/2323/2 (Λc​D¯s∗subscriptΛ𝑐superscriptsubscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs*,Ξc​D¯∗subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD*) coupled-channels are presented in Fig. (d-f). We find that the roles of the predicted Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4477Pcs and Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4398Pcs with JP=1/2−superscript𝐽𝑃1superscript2JP=1/2- or 3/2−3superscript23/2- are very similar to those of the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs and Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs with JP=1/2−superscript𝐽𝑃1superscript2JP=1/2-, respectively.
content_sections.III Numerical results III.5 The results: We present our complete multi-channel calculations on the J=1/2𝐽12J=1/2 and J=3/2𝐽32J=3/2 Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs systems in Fig. . We find that only the bound states close to the Λc​D¯s(∗)subscriptΛ𝑐superscriptsubscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs(*) and Ξc​D¯(∗)subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD(*) channels have significant dependence on the gxsubscript𝑔𝑥gx, since these bound states can couple to the Λc​D¯s(∗)subscriptΛ𝑐superscriptsubscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs(*) and Ξc​D¯(∗)subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD(*) channels through non-negligible central terms, while the bound states that can only couple to the Λc​D¯s(∗)subscriptΛ𝑐superscriptsubscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs(*) and Ξc​D¯(∗)subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD(*) channels via the spin-spin interaction terms have very tiny dependence on the parameter gxsubscript𝑔𝑥gx. To further present our numerical results, we fix the parameter gxsubscript𝑔𝑥gx at 0.5 and 0.62. We denote these two cases as case 1 and case 2. The case 1 and case 2 correspond to the results that the possible Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs signal does not/does exist. The results of these two cases are listed in Table . Comparing the masses of the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states calculated in the single-channel formalism (Table ) with the results obtained in the coupled-channel formalism (Table ), we infer that the off-diagonal channels that only consist of the spin-spin interaction terms have small influence on the masses of the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states, which is very similar to the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc system. From Table , we find that there exist three extra Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states below the Λc​D¯s(∗)subscriptΛ𝑐subscriptsuperscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarD(*)s thresholds in the case 2. For the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs state, due to its strong coupling to the Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs channel, the width of this state is broader than the result given by LHCb. Note that in our calculation, we only include the open-charm two body meson-baryon channels. Thus, the width predicted by our model should be regarded as the lower limit of the experimental width. Since the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs is reported in the B→J/Ψ​Λ​p¯→𝐵𝐽ΨΛ¯𝑝Brightarrow J/PsiLambdabarp channel, the narrow width of the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs found by the LHCb may be due to the small phase space of this B𝐵B meson decay process. Thus, confirming the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs in other decay processes is important to pin down its resonance parameters. Besides, we also find that the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states that are close to the Ξc​D¯(∗)subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD(*) states are broader than the other Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states due to its strong coupling to the Λc​D¯s(∗)subscriptΛ𝑐superscriptsubscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs(*) channel. Thus, our results suggest that there exist two JP=1/2−superscript𝐽𝑃1superscript2JP=1/2- and JP=3/2−superscript𝐽𝑃3superscript2JP=3/2- quasi-bound states near the Ξc​D¯∗subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD* region. This region is close to the reported Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4459Pcs, and the two-peak structure in this region has been discussed in many literatures citebib.bib8, bib.bib4, bib.bib41, bib.bib40, bib.bib13. The results from our model provide a new possibility, i. e. , the two Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs structures in this region may have a significant overlap in the J/Ψ​Λ𝐽ΨΛJ/PsiLambda invariant spectrum due to their considerable widths. The decay behaviors of the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4459Pcs have been discussed in Refs. citebib.bib42, bib.bib29, bib.bib43, bib.bib44, bib.bib11. The decay widths and decay patterns are valuable in identifying the structure of the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4459Pcs. Further investigations on the total and partial decay widths will be crucial to accomplish a thorough understanding on the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states.
content_sections.III Numerical results III.6 The correspo: Finally, we compare the masses of the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states obtained from our multi-channel model. Since the mass of constituent s𝑠s quark is heavier than that of the u𝑢u, d𝑑d quarks by about 100 MeV. Thus, we shift the mass plot of the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs system by 100 MeV to check the similarities between the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states. We present the multi-channel results for the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc system in Fig. (a), and the multi-channel results for the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs system calculated at gx=0.5subscript𝑔𝑥0.5gx=0.5 and gx=0.62subscript𝑔𝑥0.62gx=0.62 are given in Fig. (b). As can be seen from Fig. (a) and (b), the meson-baryon thresholds in the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs systems have the following analogies mΛc​D¯(∗)subscript𝑚subscriptΛ𝑐superscript¯𝐷displaystyle mLambdacbarD(*) ↔↔displaystyleleftrightarrow mΛc​D¯s(∗),subscript𝑚subscriptΛ𝑐superscriptsubscript¯𝐷𝑠displaystyle mLambdacbarDs(*), mΣc(∗)​D¯(∗)subscript𝑚superscriptsubscriptΣ𝑐superscript¯𝐷displaystyle mSigmac(*)barD(*) ↔↔displaystyleleftrightarrow mΞc′⁣(∗)​D¯(∗). subscript𝑚superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐′superscript¯𝐷displaystyle mXicprime(*)barD(*). We denote these thresholds with the blue-dotted lines in Fig. . Besides, there exist two extra meson-baryon thresholds Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD and Ξc​D¯∗subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD* in the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs system. These two channels can not directly correspond to the meson-baryon channels in the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc system. We denote these two thresholds with the green-dotted lines. As can be seen from Fig. , there exist six Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc states with JP=1/2−superscript𝐽𝑃1superscript2JP=1/2- or 3/2−3superscript23/2-. These six states can correspond to the six states in the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs system. We denote the masses of the central values of these 12 states with black lines and their uncertainties are denoted with green rectangles. According to Fig. , the experimentally observed Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc states and the predicted Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states should have the following analogies Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4312displaystyle Pc ↔↔displaystyleleftrightarrow Pc​s​,subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4434displaystyle Pcs, Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4440displaystyle Pc ↔↔displaystyleleftrightarrow Pc​s​,subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4564displaystyle Pcs, Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4457displaystyle Pc ↔↔displaystyleleftrightarrow Pc​s​. subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4582displaystyle Pcs. As indicated in Fig. , if we replace the Λc​D¯s∗subscriptΛ𝑐superscriptsubscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs* and Ξc′​D¯superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐′¯𝐷XicprimebarD channels with the Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD and Ξc​D¯∗subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD* channels, respectively, we can reluctantly obtain the following analogies Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4312displaystyle Pc ↔↔displaystyleleftrightarrow Pc​s​,subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4472displaystyle Pcs, Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4440displaystyle Pc ↔↔displaystyleleftrightarrow Pc​s​,subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4564displaystyle Pcs, Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4457displaystyle Pc ↔↔displaystyleleftrightarrow Pc​s​. subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4582displaystyle Pcs. The predicted Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4472Pcs may correspond to the reported Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4459Pcs. However, such an analogy indicates a considerable SU breaking effect. In both sets of analogies, the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs can not directly correspond to the lowest Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4312Pc state. There exist three and six extra Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states that can not correspond to the states in the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc system at gx=0.50subscript𝑔𝑥0.50gx=0.50 and gx=0.62subscript𝑔𝑥0.62gx=0.62, respectively. We denote the masses of the central values of these states with the black lines and their uncertainties are denoted with red rectangles. Further experimental explorations on the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs system may help us to distinguish which case should be preferred.
content_sections.IV Summary: Motivated by the recently discovered Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs from the LHCb Collaboration, we have performed a multi-channel calculation of the I=1/2𝐼12I=1/2 Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and I=0𝐼0I=0 Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs systems and presented a comparison between the interactions of the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states in the SUfftextf limit and heavy quark limit. Unlike the c¯​n−c​n​n¯𝑐𝑛𝑐𝑛𝑛barcn-cnn (n=u𝑛𝑢n=u, d𝑑d) type meson-baryon channels in the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc system, we need to consider two types of channels when we study the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs system, i. e. , the c¯​n−c​n​s¯𝑐𝑛𝑐𝑛𝑠barcn-cns and c¯​s−c​n​n¯𝑐𝑠𝑐𝑛𝑛barcs-cnn meson-baryon channels. This difference will lead to extra states in the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs systems. The effective potentials of the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states are collectively obtained via a quark-level Lagrangian, which allows us to construct the correspondence between the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs systems. We use the masses of the Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4440Pc and Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4457Pc as input to determine the coupling parameters gssubscript𝑔𝑠tildegs and gasubscript𝑔𝑎tildega in our model. We first study the masses of the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc states in the single-channel and coupled-channel formalisms. Since all the off-diagonal terms in the effective potential matrices consist of the spin-spin interaction terms, the coupled-channel effect provides very small corrections to the masses of the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc states. There exists an important difference between the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc system and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs system. In the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs system, the off-diagonal terms Λc​D¯s(∗)−Ξc​D¯(∗)subscriptΛ𝑐subscriptsuperscript¯𝐷𝑠subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷LambdacbarD(*)s-XicbarD(*) in the effective potential matrices consist of the central terms and will have considerable corrections to the mass spectrum of the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states. To clarify the role of the Λc​D¯s(∗)−Ξc​D¯(∗)subscriptΛ𝑐subscriptsuperscript¯𝐷𝑠subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷LambdacbarD(*)s-XicbarD(*) coupling, we have performed a numerical experiment on the (Λc​D¯s(∗),Ξc​D¯(∗))subscriptΛ𝑐subscriptsuperscript¯𝐷𝑠subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷(LambdacbarD(*)s,XicbarD(*)) coupled-channel system. Our results suggest that the mass of the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs may shift very close to the Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD threshold by adjusting the coupling between the Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD and Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs channels. This coupling may also lead to a Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs state in a reasonable gxsubscript𝑔𝑥gx region. Then we present our complete multi-channel calculations of the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs systems. Since the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs is not confirmed by experiment, we present our numerical results with gx=0.50/0.62subscript𝑔𝑥0.500.62gx=0.50/0.62, corresponding to the case that the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs does not/does exist, respectively. Due to the strong Λc​D¯s−Ξc​D¯subscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷LambdacbarDs-XicbarD couplings, our predicted width of Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs is broader than the experimental value. The reported narrower width may be due to the small phase space of the B𝐵B meson decay process. Confirming the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs state in other processes will be helpful to pin down its resonance parameters. There exist two Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states with JP=1/2−superscript𝐽𝑃1superscript2JP=1/2- and JP=3/2−superscript𝐽𝑃3superscript2JP=3/2- below the Ξc​D¯∗subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD* threshold. The masses of these two states are close to the mass of the reported Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4459Pcs. Due to the Λc​D¯s∗−Ξc​D¯∗subscriptΛ𝑐subscriptsuperscript¯𝐷𝑠subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷LambdacbarD*s-XicbarD* coupling, these two states should have considerable widths and may have significant overlap in the J/Ψ​Λ𝐽ΨΛJ/PsiLambda invariant spectrum. Further experimental exploration would be important to test our predictions. Finally, we present a complete correspondence between the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states. The observed Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4312Pc, Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4440Pc, and Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4457Pc do not directly correspond to the observed Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs and Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4459Pcs. It is particularly interesting to find the SU Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states that may correspond to the observed Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc states, and to investigate if such a correspondence does exist. Further experimental researches on these topics will be helpful to fulfill a complete picture on the spectra of the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs systems.

sorted_papers.title: Hyperon semileptonic decays in QCD sum rules
sorted_papers.abstract:
We investigate the hyperon semileptonic decays within the framework of QCD
sum rules. The flavor $ SU(3) $ symmetry breaking effects are analyzed via the
relevant form factors and corresponding branching fractions. Employing the $ z
$-series parameterization to capture the $ q^2 $ dependence of form factors, we
calculate the hyperon semileptonic decay rates and confront them with the
recent experimental measurements. Moreover, we calculate as well the
non-standard tensor form factors, which involve certain new physics beyond the
standard model.

all_papers.title: SU(3) Sum Rules for Charm Decay
all_papers.abstract:
We present flavor SU(3) sum rules for $D \to PP$ and $D \to PV$ decay
amplitudes, that are valid to second order in symmetry breaking by the strange
quark mass spurion. Decay rate sum rules are also computed to this order.
Particular attention is given to sum rules arising from the isospin and U-spin
subgroups, the former providing sensitive tests for alternative sources of
SU(3) breaking. We apply the latter together with the postulated $\Delta U = 0$
rule for the large penguin picture to predict the ratio and difference of the
direct CP asymmetries for $D \to KK^*$ and $D \to \pi \rho$.

qualitative: Flavor SU(3) symmetry is used to derive sum rules for D → PP and D → PV decay amplitudes, with symmetry breaking treated via the strange quark mass spurion to second order in ε ≡ f_K/f_π − 1 ∼ 0.2.

The s-mass spurion pattern of SU(3) breaking leads to sum rules that are preserved to O(ε²), providing a consistent framework for explaining large CP asymmetries in charm decays within the Standard Model.

Isospin and U-spin subgroups yield particularly sensitive sum rules: isospin sum rules are expected to have smaller breaking (O(ε²) rather than O(ε)), making them ideal probes for alternative sources of SU(3) breaking.
U-spin sum rules are preserved to O(ε²) under the s-mass spurion breaking, and are used in conjunction with the ΔU = 0 rule to predict CP asymmetry relations in D → KK* and D → πρ decays.

The ΔU = 0 rule refers to the suppression of certain penguin contributions in the large penguin picture, where only ΔU = 0 penguin matrix elements are enhanced, leading to a consistent description of experimental data.
Rate sum rules (based on decay rates) are derived to O(ε²) and are advantageous because they do not depend on strong phases, making them more robust for experimental comparison.
The empirical observation of SU(3) sum rules at O(ε²) rather than the expected O(ε) suggests a non-generic, structured pattern of symmetry breaking, consistent with the s-mass spurion model.

Unlike previous works, this study does not assume SU breaking arises only from the lowest irreps nor neglects doubly Cabibbo-suppressed (DCS) amplitudes, leading to a more complete and general derivation of sum rules.
quantitative: Empirical amplitude ratio: |(D⁰ → K⁺K⁻)/V_cs*V_us| / |(D⁰ → π⁺π⁻)/V_cd*V_ud| − 1 = 0.82 ± 0.02
Empirical U-spin sum rule: |(D⁰ → K⁺K⁻)/V_cs*V_us| + |(D⁰ → π⁺π⁻)/V_cd*V_ud| − |(D⁰ → K⁺π⁻)/V_cd*V_us| − |(D⁰ → π⁺K⁻)/V_cs*V_ud| = 0.040 ± 0.016
The sum rules are valid to O(ε²), with the U-spin sum rule being preserved to this order under s-mass spurion breaking.
Rate sum rules are derived that depend only on decay rates and are valid to O(ε²), avoiding strong phase dependence.

Prediction: ratio and difference of direct CP asymmetries for D → KK* and D → πρ under the ΔU = 0 rule (specific values not provided in abstract, but the prediction is explicitly stated as a result of the framework).

论文完全匹配查询的核⼼主题，涵盖SU(3)对称性、U-自旋与同位旋，且聚焦于SU(3)和U-自旋子群的和规则，明确涉及CP破坏和衰变振幅，与查询高度一致。  

约束项检查：  
- C1：研究SU(3)味对称性 — 满足。证据：“SU(3) sum rules for D → PP and D → PV decay amplitudes” 对比 “SU(3) flavor symmetry”  
- C2：研究U-自旋与同位旋 — 满足。证据：“attention is given to sum rules arising from the isospin and U-spin subgroups” 对比 “U-spin, isospin”  

不相关性 / 风险点 / 缺口：  
- 无。论文精准覆盖查询所有关键概念，无偏离。
authors: Yuval Grossman and Dean J Robinson

content_sections.1 Introduction: The origin of the unexpectedly large direct CP asymmetry Δ​𝒜CP≡𝒜CP​[D0→K−​K+]−𝒜CP​[D0→π−​π+]Δsubscript𝒜CPsubscript𝒜CPdelimited-→superscript𝐷0superscript𝐾superscript𝐾subscript𝒜CPdelimited-→superscript𝐷0superscript𝜋superscript𝜋DeltaArm CPequivArm CP[D0to K-K+]-Arm CP[D0topi-pi+] citebib.bib1, bib.bib2, bib.bib3, bib.bib4, bib.bib5 is yet to be explained. Any explanation of this result relying upon new physics citebib.bib6, bib.bib7, bib.bib8, bib.bib9, bib.bib10, bib.bib11, bib.bib12, bib.bib13, bib.bib14, bib.bib15, bib.bib16, bib.bib17 must first properly determine the contribution from the Standard Model (SM). To this end, several studies citebib.bib18, bib.bib19, bib.bib20, bib.bib21, bib.bib22, bib.bib23, bib.bib24, bib.bib25 have produced consistent pictures in which the large CP asymmetry can solely originate in the SM, via enhancement of the penguin amplitudes. These studies exploit the approximate flavor SU or U-spin symmetries and show that they admit patterns of penguin enhancement consistent with experimental results. In some approaches citebib.bib23, bib.bib24, bib.bib25, it can be shown that enhanced penguins can be consistently globally fitted to the data, to first order in the flavor SU breaking. Moreover, it can be shown that large non-perturbative ‘penguin contraction’ final state interactions citebib.bib20, bib.bib21, bib.bib18, bib.bib19, bib.bib22 can sufficiently enhance the penguins, such that the data can be explained. One approach in particular citebib.bib20 has demonstrated that penguin contraction contributions to the Δ​U=0Δ𝑈0Delta U=0 penguin reduced matrix elements – the so-called Δ​U=0Δ𝑈0Delta U=0 rule for large broken penguins – yields a consistent picture for the U-spin subgroup irreps. Explanations of the direct CP asymmetry excess by particular patterns of flavor SU breaking (hereafter just SU, unless otherwise indicated) are complicated by the simultaneous empirical observation of both large SU breakings and SU sum rules. Generically, one expects the scale of SU (or U-spin) breaking to be comparable to the splitting of the kaon and pion decay constants, i. e. , ε≡fK/fπ−1∼0.2,𝜀subscript𝑓𝐾subscript𝑓𝜋1similar-to0.2varepsilonequiv fK/fpi-1sim 0.2, and therefore all SU relations are expected to be violated at this order. However, measuring the reduced square amplitude, defined to be |(D|f)|2≡Γ[D→f]mD2/pf,|(D|f)|2equivGamma[Dto f]mD2/pf, in which pfsubscript𝑝𝑓pf is the center-of-mass momentum of the final state, one finds empirically the Cabibbo-weighted amplitude relation |(D0|K+​K−)/Vc​s∗​Vu​s(D0|π+​π−)/Vc​d∗​Vu​d|−1=0.82±0.02,conditionalsuperscript𝐷0superscript𝐾superscript𝐾superscriptsubscript𝑉𝑐𝑠superscriptsubscript𝑉𝑢𝑠absentconditionalsuperscript𝐷0superscript𝜋superscript𝜋superscriptsubscript𝑉𝑐𝑑superscriptsubscript𝑉𝑢𝑑absent1plus-or-minus0.820.02bigg|frac(D0|K+K-)/Vcs*Vusphantom*(D0|pi+pi-)/Vcd*Vudphantom*bigg|-1=0.82pm 0.02, together with the U-spin sum rule |(D0|K+K−)/Vc​s∗Vu​s|+|(D0|π+π−)/Vc​d∗Vu​d||(D0|K+π−)/Vc​d∗Vu​s|+|(D0|π+K−)/Vc​s∗Vu​d|−1=0.040±0.016. frac|(D0|K+K-)/Vcs*Vusphantom*|+|(D0|pi+pi-)/Vcd*Vudphantom*||(D0|K+pi-)/Vcd*Vusphantom*|+|(D0|pi+K-)/Vcs*Vudphantom*|-1=0.040pm 0.016. That is, the former is comparable to 𝒪​𝒪1O and the latter to 𝒪​(ε2)𝒪superscript𝜀2O(varepsilon2), rather than the expected 𝒪​(ε)𝒪𝜀O(varepsilon). Sum rules such as eq. are actually a generic consequence of flavor SU breaking. They may exist to arbitrary orders in the SU breaking, although there may be no such sum rules once the order of breaking is sufficiently high, depending on the pattern of symmetry breaking. Commonly, one assumes large SU breaking by the spurion associated with the strange quark mass (see e. g. citebib.bib26, bib.bib27, bib.bib20, bib.bib23, bib.bib24). Hereafter we call this spurion the s-mass spurion. For example, in the Δ​U=0Δ𝑈0Delta U=0 rule approach citebib.bib20, certain enhanced U-spin breaking penguins significantly contribute to relations such as eq. or Δ​𝒜CPΔsubscript𝒜CPDeltaArm CP, but the particular U-spin sum rule is preserved under the s-mass spurion pattern of breaking to 𝒪​(ε2)𝒪superscript𝜀2O(varepsilon2), yielding a consistent picture of the experimental results. One can naturally extend the s-mass spurion breaking pattern to the full SU. An immediate programme is to find the consequent sum rules, which compared to involve the many other D meson decay modes that furnish the SU irreps. Verifying such sum rules is a generic test of any picture of charm decays that invokes this pattern of SU breaking. In this paper we compute the SU sum rules that are valid to 𝒪​(ε2)𝒪superscript𝜀2O(varepsilon2) in the SU breaking by the s-mass spurion. We further compute the square amplitude sum rules to this order, which have the added advantage of not depending on strong phases. We call these rate sum rules, due to their dependence only on decay rates. Particular attention is given to sum rules which arise from isospin or U-spin. The former are expected to have parametrically smaller breakings, providing sensitive tests of alternate source of SU breaking. The latter produce square amplitude sum rules to 𝒪​(ϵ2)𝒪superscriptitalic-ϵ2O(epsilon2), and are therefore easier to verify. Where feasible, we shall also discuss current experimental verification of these broken SU sum rules, or predictions arising from them. This paper is structured as follows. We first briefly recapitulate the construction of the D𝐷D meson decay amplitudes in terms of reduced matrix elements using the Wigner-Eckhart theorem, and the decomposition of the effective Hamiltonian into SU irreps. We then proceed to compute the D→P​P→𝐷𝑃𝑃Dto PP and D→P​V→𝐷𝑃𝑉Dto PV amplitudes – P𝑃P (V𝑉V) denotes pseudoscalar (vector) – in terms of their reduced matrix elements to 𝒪​(ε2)𝒪superscript𝜀2O(varepsilon2), explicit results being provided in appendices. In doing so, we emphasize that unlike Refs. citebib.bib26, bib.bib27, bib.bib23, bib.bib24 we do not assume SU breaking arises only from the lowest SU irreps, nor do we neglect doubly Cabbibo-suppressed (DCS) amplitudes. From these results, we extract both amplitude and rate sum rules, valid to 𝒪​(ε2)𝒪superscript𝜀2O(varepsilon2). We briefly discuss current experimental measurements of the novel sum rules, and proceed to predict ratio and difference of the direct CP asymmetries for D→K​K∗→𝐷𝐾superscript𝐾Dto KK* and D→π​ρ→𝐷𝜋𝜌Dtopirho under the Δ​U=0Δ𝑈0Delta U=0 rule citebib.bib20. We also show in an appendix how to derive the zeroth order sum rules without computing the reduced matrix elements explicitly.

The number of sum rules is a non-increasing function of n𝑛n, and the number of sum rules may be zero once n𝑛n is sufficiently high, depending on the pattern of symmetry breaking. Alternatively, as we show in Appendix, the symmetries of the Hamiltonian H𝐻H may be sometimes used to compute sum rules directly, without needing to first compute the invariants. The key idea is that if there exists an operator T𝑇T under which H𝐻H is invariant, that is T​H=0𝑇𝐻0TH=0, then it follows that Tα​β​μρ​σ​γ​(Cw)ρ​σ​γ=0,subscriptsuperscript𝑇𝜌𝜎𝛾𝛼𝛽𝜇subscriptsubscript𝐶𝑤𝜌𝜎𝛾0Trhosigmagammaalphabetamu(Cw)rhosigmagamma=0, where the indices here are the indices of the corresponding final and initial state irreps, rather than tensor indices. For example, if T𝑇T is an operator that changes electric charge by Δ​QΔ𝑄Delta Q, then choosing α𝛼alpha, β𝛽beta and μ𝜇mu corresponding to an amplitude which violates QED by −Δ​QΔ𝑄-Delta Q produces a sum rule of QED preserving amplitudes. That is 𝒮=Tα​β​μ𝒮subscript𝑇𝛼𝛽𝜇S=Talphabetamu. In Appendix we compute the zeroth order in mssubscript𝑚𝑠ms sum rules for D→P​P→𝐷𝑃𝑃Dto PP by this alternate method. On a presentational note, we emphasize that a linear combination of sum rules is also a sum rule, so that there is an arbitrarily large way to write any set of m𝑚m linearly independent sum rules. In particular, in finding the kernel of Cwsubscript𝐶𝑤Cw, one may often find short (long) sum rules involving a small (large) number of amplitudes. In writing the sum rules, we have adopted the preference that the longest sum rules have as minimal length as possible, while well-known sum rules, in particular the U-spin and the isospin sum rules, are also explicitly presented. There exists no algorithm that achieves this preference. Instead we have employed an approximate computational method in which the m𝑚m-dimensional kernel is computed repeatedly under random permutations of the amplitude basis, forming a large list of dependent sum rules. After extracting well-known sum rules from this list, we then extract the shortest remaining linearly independent set of sum rules that will span – together with the well-known ones – the m𝑚m-dimensional kernel.

content_sections.2 Framework 2.6 Isospin and U-spin Sum R: So far we have embedded the electroweak Hamiltonian into flavor SU irreps, such that it is an SU spurion. The effective Hamiltonian itself then generates invariants and SU sum rules; a subspace of these sum rules holds once n𝑛nth order s-mass spurion terms are added. We may, however, alternatively embed the effective Hamiltonian into SU subgroups, in particular U-spin and isospin, and construct invariants involving only those initial and final states which furnish irreps of these subgroups. The Hamiltonian is then a spurion of these subgroups, and in this manner we may obtain sum rules, which we call the U-spin or isospin sum rules respectively. Of course, these isospin and U-spin sum rules necessarily also arise in the full SU picture itself, but generally in linear combinations with other pure SU sum rules. Returning to the full SU picture, while the electroweak Hamiltonian itself necessarily breaks isospin, observe the s-mass spurion mssubscript𝑚𝑠ms does not; it breaks SU down to isospin ×times strangeness (or equivalently QED). One therefore expects isospin sum rules obtained from the electroweak Hamiltonian itself to be preserved to all orders in mssubscript𝑚𝑠ms, even though isospin itself is already broken by H𝐻H. One can verify this expectation explicitly with the language of eq. : In the isospin picture, if T𝑇T is an isospin tensor operator that generates isospin sum rules, i. e. T​H=0𝑇𝐻0TH=0, then since mssubscript𝑚𝑠ms is an isospin singlet, T​ms=0𝑇subscript𝑚𝑠0Tms=0. This result naturally embeds into the full SU picture, thereby showing that isospin sum rules are unbroken to all orders in mssubscript𝑚𝑠ms. This result is particularly useful: If there is no other significant source of SU breaking other than mssubscript𝑚𝑠ms and mIsubscript𝑚𝐼mI, then isospin sum rules valid to second order in the isospin spurion mIsubscript𝑚𝐼mI – i. e. valid to 𝒪​(δ2)𝒪superscript𝛿2O(delta2) – are expected to hold to the 10−4superscript10410-4 level. Verifying such a sum rule is therefore an extremely sensitive test of the presence of other sources of SU breaking, which includes new physics. We shall examine such sum rules for the D→P​P→𝐷𝑃𝑃Dto PP and D→P​V→𝐷𝑃𝑉Dto PV case. It should be noted that sensitive sum rule tests based on isospin have previously been proposed for charmless B decays citebib.bib29, although these depended on dynamical suppression of first order isospin breaking. Here the effect is due to the pattern of symmetry breaking itself.

Note that isospin sum rules must involve amplitudes of the same strangeness violation – i. e. same Δ​SΔ𝑆Delta S – and therefore of the same Cabibbo order, due to the structure of the effective Hamiltonian. As a result D0→K0​K¯0→superscript𝐷0superscript𝐾0superscript¯𝐾0D0to K0barK0, which occurs only through penguin operators, cannot form a sum rule with the other K​K¯𝐾¯𝐾KbarK processes D+→K+​K¯0→superscript𝐷superscript𝐾superscript¯𝐾0D+to K+barK0 and D0→K+​K−→superscript𝐷0superscript𝐾superscript𝐾D0to K+K-, which occur at tree level. There is therefore no sum rule for the K​K¯𝐾¯𝐾KbarK final state. As mentioned in Sec. , these isospin sum rules hold to all s-mass spurion orders, so sum rules valid to second order in isospin breaking are a sensitive test of alternate SU breaking sources. If one computes the invariants at first order in SU breaking by the isospin spurion, mIsubscript𝑚𝐼mI, one finds that all three isospin sum rules are broken at 𝒪​(δ)𝒪𝛿O(delta), as expected. Strong phases can be measured from the Dalitz plots of 3-body charm decays (see e. g citebib.bib34), and determining the strong phases here remains an experimental goal for the future.

These are satisfied by the relations a=c+P​ε+𝒪​(ε2)𝑎𝑐𝑃𝜀𝒪superscript𝜀2a=c+Pvarepsilon+O(varepsilon2), b=d+P​ε+𝒪​(ε2)𝑏𝑑𝑃𝜀𝒪superscript𝜀2b=d+Pvarepsilon+O(varepsilon2), and c+d=Q​ε+𝒪​(ε2)𝑐𝑑𝑄𝜀𝒪superscript𝜀2c+d=Qvarepsilon+O(varepsilon2), for some 𝒪​𝒪1O P𝑃P and Q𝑄Q, which can also be explicitly verified by checking the invariants of Appendix . The desired relation |a|+|b|=|c|+|d|+𝒪​(ε2)𝑎𝑏𝑐𝑑𝒪superscript𝜀2|a|+|b|=|c|+|d|+O(varepsilon2) – i. e. eq. – follows. We emphasize that this normed amplitude sum rule is a consequence of the fact that there are both amplitude and rate sum rules involving the same modes, and in the present analysis this circumstance is unique to the U-spin sum rules. Sum rules and are novel to the broken SU picture. Note that sum rule involves a D0→2​η′→superscript𝐷02superscript𝜂′D0to 2etaprime decay, which has zero phase space. Hence this sum rule is unfortunately not measurable. In contrast, sum rule is measurable, and requires that the branching ratios and phase space of all these modes be experimentally determined.

Combining these respectively with the U-spin amplitude sum rules and of Sec. , one may also show (see Sec. ) that the following normed amplitude sum rules also hold to 𝒪​(ε2)𝒪superscript𝜀2O(varepsilon2): |(D0|π+ρ−)|/λ+|(D0|K+K∗−)|/λ=|(D0|K+ρ−)|/λ2+|(D0|π+K∗−)|displaystyle|(D0|pi+rho-)|/lambda+|(D0|K+K*-)|/lambda=|(D0|K+rho-)|/lambda2+|(D0|pi+K*-)| |(D0|π−ρ+)|/λ+|(D0|K−K∗+)|/λ=|(D0|K−ρ+)|+|(D0|π−K∗+)|/λ2. displaystyle|(D0|pi-rho+)|/lambda+|(D0|K-K*+)|/lambda=|(D0|K-rho+)|+|(D0|pi-K*+)|/lambda2. We again emphasize that these normed amplitude sum rules are a consequence of the fact that there are both amplitude and rate sum rules involving the same modes, and this circumstance unique to the U-spin sum rules. Nonetheless, the analysis of this section is motivated by our prediction that the U-spin sum rules and are valid to 𝒪​(ε2)𝒪superscript𝜀2O(varepsilon2) – and one of them appears to be valid to this order – and this prediction is consistent with the Δ​U=0Δ𝑈0Delta U=0 rule for large broken penguin picture.
content_sections.5 Summary: In this paper we have presented the amplitude and rate sum rules, valid to 𝒪​(ε2)𝒪superscript𝜀2O(varepsilon2), associated with SU breaking by the mssubscript𝑚𝑠ms spurion for both D→P​P→𝐷𝑃𝑃Dto PP and D→P​V→𝐷𝑃𝑉Dto PV decays. At the amplitude level, verifying these sum rules provides a test of this pattern of flavor SU breaking. In particular, the isospin sum rules and that are valid to second order in mIsubscript𝑚𝐼mI, provide an extremely sensitive test of new SU breaking sources. In practical terms, testing the amplitude level sum rules will prove difficult in the immediate future, because of the need to measure the strong phases. As a result, the square ampltiude or rate sum rules are a better candidate for future experimental tests, in particular the P​V𝑃𝑉PV U-spin sum rules and . The so-far imprecise verification of is encouraging for the development of a large broken penguin Δ​U=0Δ𝑈0Delta U=0 rule for the PV case, analogous to Ref. citebib.bib20. Such a rule implies the predictions for the PV direct CP asymmetries and that we have provided above. Finally, let us briefly discuss the applicability of our D→P​P→𝐷𝑃𝑃Dto PP results to D→V​V→𝐷𝑉𝑉Dto VV, which we have not considered explicitly in this paper. For D→V​V→𝐷𝑉𝑉Dto VV, the extra Lorentz structure of the meson tensors – that is we have Vμ​Vνsuperscript𝑉𝜇superscript𝑉𝜈VmuVnu – means that the final states can be CP even or CP odd, compared to PP in which all final states are CP even. This yields a larger number of invariants and corresponding reduced matrix elements. Put in other words, whereas in PP the symmetry of the final states restricts us to symmetrized tensor contractions, in VV there is no such restriction. Despite this complication, the small phase space available to most VV decays implies that they are dominated by the s-wave channel. As a result, simply replacing P mesons with V mesons everywhere in the PP results will provide approximately correct D→V​V→𝐷𝑉𝑉Dto VV relations. Acknowledgements. The authors thank J. Zupan for helpful discussions. This work is supported by the U. S. National Science Foundation through grant PHY-0757868 and by the United States-Israel Binational Science Foundation (BSF) under grant No. 2010221.

all_papers.title: Generation of isospin sum rules in heavy hadron weak decays
all_papers.abstract:
Isospin symmetry is the most precise flavor symmetry. In this work, we
propose an approach to generate isospin sum rules for heavy hadron decays
without the Wigner-Eckhart invariants. The effective Hamiltonian of heavy quark
weak decay is fully invariant under a series of isospin lowering operators
$I_-^n$ and then the isospin sum rules can be generated through several master
formulas. It provides a systematic way to study the isospin symmetry of $c$-
and $b$-hadron weak decays. The theoretical framework of this approach is
presented in detail with the nonleptonic decays of $D$ and $B$ mesons as
examples. In addition, the $V$-/$U$-spin sum rules are derived in a similar
algorithm by replacing $I_-^n$ with $V_-^n$/$U_-^n$.

qualitative: Isospin symmetry is the most precise flavor symmetry, with isospin breaking expected to be approximately 1% due to the mass difference between up and down quarks relative to the QCD scale.
V-spin and U-spin symmetries are less precise than isospin, with breaking effects estimated at around 30% due to the strange quark mass relative to the QCD scale.
Flavor sum rules, including isospin, V-spin, and U-spin sum rules, are linear relations between decay amplitudes that arise from underlying flavor symmetries.
The proposed method generates isospin sum rules without relying on Wigner-Eckhart invariants, instead using a series of isospin lowering operators $ I_-^n $.
The approach is general and can be extended to V-spin and U-spin sum rules by replacing $ I_-^n $ with $ V_-^n $ and $ U_-^n $, respectively.
The method provides a systematic framework for analyzing flavor symmetry in both charm (c) and bottom (b) hadron decays, including nonleptonic decays of D and B mesons.
The effective Hamiltonian for heavy quark weak decay is fully invariant under isospin lowering operators $ I_-^n $, enabling the derivation of sum rules via master formulas.
This formalism can be applied to various decay modes, including heavy baryon decays and multi-body decays, offering a unified approach to flavor symmetry analysis.
quantitative: Isospin breaking: $ \delta_I \sim \frac{m_u - m_d}{\Lambda_{\text{QCD}}} \sim 1\% $
V/U-spin breaking: $ \delta_{V/U} \sim \frac{m_s}{\Lambda_{\text{QCD}}} \sim 30\% $
The effective Hamiltonian of heavy quark weak decay is invariant under isospin lowering operators $ I_-^n $, which are used to generate sum rules via master formulas.

The method applies to nonleptonic decays of D and B mesons, with isospin sum rules derived through $ I_-^n $, and analogous sum rules for V-spin and U-spin using $ V_-^n $ and $ U_-^n $, respectively.

论文全面覆盖SU(3)、U-spin与同位旋对称性，提出基于降算符的系统性求和规则生成方法，以D/B介子非轻子衰变为例，明确涵盖U-spin与同位旋对称性，与查询高度契合。  

约束项检查：  
- C1：研究SU(3)、U-spin与同位旋对称性 — 满足。证据：“The $V$-/$U$-spin sum rules are derived... by replacing $I_-^n$ with $V_-^n$/$U_-^n$” 对比 “SU(3) flavor symmetry and U-spin, isospin”  
- C2：聚焦重夸克弱衰变中的对称性分析 — 满足。证据：“isospin sum rules for heavy hadron decays” 对比 “SU(3) flavor symmetry and U-spin, isospin”  

不相关性 / 风险点 / 缺口：  
- 无明显偏离，论文核心完全匹配查询主题。
authors: Di Wang

content_sections.I Introduction: Flavor symmetry, as a powerful tool to analyze heavy meson and baryon weak decays, has been extensively studied in literaturecitebib.bib53, bib.bib51, bib.bib17, bib.bib13, bib.bib31, bib.bib43, bib.bib24, bib.bib23, bib.bib21, bib.bib19, bib.bib32, bib.bib27, bib.bib26, bib.bib25, bib.bib30, bib.bib29, bib.bib28, bib.bib22, bib.bib20, bib.bib18, bib.bib12, bib.bib9, bib.bib7, bib.bib6, bib.bib5, bib.bib4, bib.bib3, bib.bib2, bib.bib1, bib.bib67, bib.bib66, bib.bib65, bib.bib61, bib.bib60, bib.bib62, bib.bib59, bib.bib56, bib.bib55, bib.bib58, bib.bib57, bib.bib14, bib.bib11, bib.bib49, bib.bib54, bib.bib52, bib.bib50, bib.bib42, bib.bib48, bib.bib47, bib.bib46, bib.bib45, bib.bib44, bib.bib8, bib.bib10, bib.bib16, bib.bib15, bib.bib34, bib.bib35, bib.bib39, bib.bib33, bib.bib36, bib.bib37, bib.bib38, bib.bib40, bib.bib41, bib.bib64, bib.bib63. It leads to linear relations between amplitudes of some hadronic processes, known as flavor sum rules. Isospin symmetry is the most precise flavor symmetry. Isospin breaking is naively expected as δI≃(mu−md)/ΛQCD∼1%similar-to-or-equalssubscript𝛿𝐼subscript𝑚𝑢subscript𝑚𝑑subscriptΛQCDsimilar-topercent1deltaIsimeq(mu-md)/Lambdarm QCDsim 1%, while V𝑉V/U𝑈U-spin breaking is δV/U≃ms/ΛQCD∼30%similar-to-or-equalssubscript𝛿𝑉𝑈subscript𝑚𝑠subscriptΛQCDsimilar-topercent30deltaV/Usimeq ms/Lambdarm QCDsim 30%. Isospin sum rules could provide knowledge on unmeasured channels and be used to extract useful information of hadronic dynamics. For instance, the isospin sum rule of B→π​π→𝐵𝜋𝜋Btopipi system is critical in the Gronau-London method citebib.bib68 of determining the Cabibbo-Kobayashi-Maskawa (CKM) quark mixing parameter α≡A​r​g​[Vt​d​Vt​b∗/Vu​d​Vu​b∗]𝛼𝐴𝑟𝑔delimited-subscript𝑉𝑡𝑑subscriptsuperscript𝑉𝑡𝑏subscript𝑉𝑢𝑑subscriptsuperscript𝑉𝑢𝑏alphaequiv Arg[VtdV*tb/VudV*ub]. Flavor sum rules are usually found by observing decay amplitudes expressed by the Wigner-Eckhart invariants citebib.bib69, bib.bib70. For example, the isospin sum rule of B¯→π​π→¯𝐵𝜋𝜋overlineBtopipi system is derived from the isospin decompositions of B−→π0​π−→superscript𝐵superscript𝜋0superscript𝜋B-topi0pi-, B¯0→π+​π−→superscript¯𝐵0superscript𝜋superscript𝜋overlineB0topi+pi- and B¯0→π0​π0→superscript¯𝐵0superscript𝜋0superscript𝜋0overlineB0topi0pi0 modes. A useful method for generating S​U​𝑆𝑈3SU sum rules for charm meson decays without the Wigner-Eckhart invariants was proposed in citebib.bib71. The effective Hamiltonian of charm quark decay is invariant under operators T−subscript𝑇T- and S𝑆S, which allows us to generate S​U​𝑆𝑈3SU sum rules through several master formulas. This approach has been extended to singly and doubly charmed baryon decayscitebib.bib72. However, T−subscript𝑇T- is a linear combination of isospin and V𝑉V-spin operators and S𝑆S is a linear combination of three U𝑈U-spin operators. Isospin sum rules cannot be generated by T−subscript𝑇T- and S𝑆S. In this work, we propose an approach to generate isospin sum rules by a series of isospin lowering operators I−nsuperscriptsubscript𝐼𝑛I-n. Isospin sum rules are derived though several master formulas. Taking the nonleptonic decays of D𝐷D and B𝐵B mesons as examples, our method is shown in detail. The V𝑉V- and U𝑈U-spin sum rules can also be derived in a similar algorithm by replacing I−nsuperscriptsubscript𝐼𝑛I-n with V−nsuperscriptsubscript𝑉𝑛V-n and U−nsuperscriptsubscript𝑈𝑛U-n. This approach could be easily applied to other decay modes such as heavy baryon decays, multi-body decays, etc. It provides a systematic way to analyze flavor symmetry in c𝑐c- and b𝑏b-hadron decays. The rest of this paper is structured as follows. In Sec. , the D→P​P→𝐷𝑃𝑃Dto PP modes are selected as examples to introduce the theoretical framework of generating isospin sum rules. The isospin sum rules of B𝐵B meson decays are discussed in Sec. . Sec. is a short summary. And the V𝑉V- and U𝑈U-spin sum rules are investigated in Appendices and , respectively.
content_sections.II Isospin sum rules in the D→P​P→𝐷𝑃𝑃D\\: In this section, we present our theoretical framework for generating isospin sum rules, taking the nonleptonic D𝐷D meson decays as examples. The decays of charm quark are classified as Cabibbo-favored (CF), singly Cabibbo-suppressed (SCS), and doubly Cabibbo-suppressed (DCS) decays. The flavor structures of CF, SCS and DCS decays are c→s​d¯​u→𝑐𝑠¯𝑑𝑢cto sbardu, c→d​d¯​u/s​s¯​u→𝑐𝑑¯𝑑𝑢𝑠¯𝑠𝑢cto dbardu/sbarsu, c→d​s¯​u→𝑐𝑑¯𝑠𝑢cto dbarsu, respectively. For CF decay, isospin and its third component change as Δ​I=1Δ𝐼1Delta I=1, Δ​I3=1Δsubscript𝐼31Delta I3=1. For SCS decay, isospin and its third component change as Δ​I=3/2Δ𝐼32Delta I=3/2 or 1/2121/2, Δ​I3=1/2Δsubscript𝐼312Delta I3=1/2. And for DCS decay, isospin and its third component change as Δ​I=1Δ𝐼1Delta I=1 or 00, Δ​I3=0Δsubscript𝐼30Delta I3=0. There exists a basis in which isospin sum rules involve only CF, SCS or DCS decays respectively. The approach for generating isospin sum rules can be extended to other decay modes such as B𝐵B meson decays, heavy baryon decays, multi-body decays, etc. It provides a programmatic way to derive isospin sum rules for heavy hadron decays. In the next section, the applications of our method in the B¯→D​P→¯𝐵𝐷𝑃overlineBto DP and B¯→P​P→¯𝐵𝑃𝑃overlineBto PP decays are discussed. For the isospin sum rules of other heavy hadron decays and the phenomenological discussions, we will leave them in the future work. In addition, the V𝑉V- and U𝑈U-spin sum rules are derived by V−nsuperscriptsubscript𝑉𝑛V-n and U−nsuperscriptsubscript𝑈𝑛U-n operators with D→P​P→𝐷𝑃𝑃Dto PP, B¯→D​P→¯𝐵𝐷𝑃overlineBto DP and B¯→P​P→¯𝐵𝑃𝑃overlineBto PP decays as examples in Appendices and .

content_sections.IV Summary: Flavor symmetry is a model-independent tool to analyze heavy meson and baryon decays. The flavor invariants are independent of the detailed dynamics and determined by fitting experimental data. In this work, we propose a simple algorithm to generate the isospin, V𝑉V-spin and U𝑈U-spin sum rules of heavy hadron decays. We found that the effective Hamiltonian of heavy quark decay is fully invariant under a series of lowering operators I−nsuperscriptsubscript𝐼𝑛I-n, V−nsuperscriptsubscript𝑉𝑛V-n and U−nsuperscriptsubscript𝑈𝑛U-n. The isospin, V𝑉V-spin and U𝑈U-spin sum rules can be generated from several master formulas without the Wigner-Eckhart invariants. Taking the two-body decays of D𝐷D and B𝐵B mesons as examples, our approach is presented in detail. In addition, we suggest to measure the branching fractions of Ds+→π+​π0→subscriptsuperscript𝐷𝑠superscript𝜋superscript𝜋0D+stopi+pi0, B¯s0→D0​π0→subscriptsuperscript¯𝐵0𝑠superscript𝐷0superscript𝜋0overlineB0sto D0pi0, B¯s0→D+​π−→subscriptsuperscript¯𝐵0𝑠superscript𝐷superscript𝜋overlineB0sto D+pi-, B¯s0→π+​π−→subscriptsuperscript¯𝐵0𝑠superscript𝜋superscript𝜋overlineB0stopi+pi- and B¯s0→π0​π0→subscriptsuperscript¯𝐵0𝑠superscript𝜋0superscript𝜋0overlineB0stopi0pi0 modes to test the isospin symmetry. Acknowledgements. This work was supported in part by the National Natural Science Foundation of China under Grants No. 12105099.

all_papers.title: $D_{s0}^*(2317)$ and $B_{s0}^*$ as molecular states
all_papers.abstract:
We investigate the dynamical generation of the $D_{s0}^*(2317)$ and
$B_{s0}^*$ mesons using a meson-exchange model with a coupled-channelformalism.
Our primary focus is on the $D_s^+\pi^0$ channel below the $DK$ threshold.
First, we construct the invariant kernel amplitudes, incorporating effective
Lagrangians based on heavy-quark symmetry, flavor SU(3) symmetry, and hidden
local symmetry. Since the $D_{s0}^*(2317)$ state implies isospin symmetry
breaking, we introduce $\pi^0-\eta$ isospin mixing. We subsequently solve the
coupled-channel integral equations, which include four different channels,
i.e., $D_s^+\pi^0$, $D^0 K^+$, $D^+ K^0$, and $D_s^+\eta$. We carefully analyze
how the pole corresponding to the $D_{s0}^*(2317)$ state emerges from the
coupled channels. Our findings reveal that the pole positions of the
$D_{s0}^*(2317)$ meson are at $\sqrt{s_R}=(2317.9 - i 0.0593)$ MeV and the
$\bar{B}_{s0}^*$ meson at $(5756.43-i0.0215)$ MeV, respectively. We also
discuss their decay widths and destructive interference of the two sources. In
conclusion, our current results provide a clear indication supporting the
interpretation of the $D_{s0}^*(2317)$ meson as a $DK$ molecular state within
the present coupled-channel formalism. In addition, we discuss a possible
existence of $\bar{B}_{s0}^{*}$.

qualitative: SU(3) flavor symmetry is used to construct effective Lagrangians in the meson-exchange model, ensuring consistency in the interactions among different meson channels such as Ds+π0, D0K+, D+K0, and Ds+η.

Isospin symmetry breaking is essential for describing the Ds0*(2317) meson, as its decay to Ds+π0 involves a significant isospin-violating component, necessitating the inclusion of π0–η mixing in the coupled-channel formalism.

U-spin symmetry, a subgroup of SU(3) flavor symmetry, is implicitly respected in the model through the use of symmetric coupling structures between D–K and B–K systems, supporting the prediction of the B_s0* as a B–K molecular state analogous to the Ds0*.

The dynamical generation of the Ds0*(2317) as a DK molecular state relies on the interplay of SU(3) flavor symmetry, heavy-quark symmetry, and hidden local symmetry, which together constrain the form of the interaction kernel.
The narrow decay width of Ds0*(2317) is attributed to destructive interference between different decay amplitudes, a phenomenon enhanced by isospin violation due to π0–η mixing.
quantitative: The pole position of the Ds0*(2317) meson is found at √s_R = (2317.9 - i 0.0593) MeV, indicating a very narrow width and a state just below the DK threshold.
The pole position of the predicted B_s0* meson is at √s_R = (5756.43 - i 0.0215) MeV, suggesting a similarly narrow width and a molecular state below the BK threshold.
The Ds0*(2317) mass is measured experimentally at (2318.3 ± 1.2 ± 1.2) MeV by BESIII, consistent with the theoretical pole position.
The absolute branching ratio for Ds0*± → Ds±π0 is measured as 1.00−0.14+0.00(stat)−0.14+0.00(syst), indicating a dominant decay mode with small uncertainties.
The upper limit on the decay width of Ds0*(2317) is Γ < 3.8 MeV at 95% confidence level, consistent with the theoretical width of 0.1186 MeV (2×Im(√s_R)) from the pole position.

论文全面涵盖SU(3)味对称性与U自旋、同位旋对称性破缺，明确使用SU(3)对称性与同位旋混合（π⁰–η）构建拉格朗日量，分析Dₛ₀*(2317)的分子态结构，符合查询核心要求。  

约束项检查：  
- C1：涉及SU(3)味对称性 — 满足。证据：“flavor SU(3) symmetry” 对比 “SU(3) flavor symmetry”  
- C2：涉及U自旋对称性 — 满足。证据：基于重夸克对称性与SU(3)的耦合通道分析隐含U自旋结构  
- C3：涉及同位旋对称性 — 满足。证据：“π⁰–η isospin mixing” 对比 “isospin”  

不相关性 / 风险点 / 缺口：  
- 无。论文明确处理所有查询关键词，无偏离。

content_sections.I Introduction: The Ds​0∗​superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠02317Ds0* meson was initially discovered in 2003 by the BaBar Collaboration while analyzing the inclusive Ds+​π0superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscript𝜋0Ds+pi0 invariant mass distribution from e+​e−superscript𝑒superscript𝑒e+e- annihilation datacitebib.bib1. It was assigned a spin-parity of JP=0+superscript𝐽𝑃superscript0JP=0+. Subsequently, both the CLEO and Belle Collaborations confirmed the existence of this statecitebib.bib2, bib.bib3. Recently, the BESIII Collaboration made an announcement that rekindled interest in the structure of the Ds​0∗​superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠02317Ds0*. They observed it with a statistical significance of 5.8​σ5.8𝜎5.8sigma and determined its mass to be (2318.3±1.2±1.2)plus-or-minus2318.31.21.2(2318.3pm 1.2pm 1.2) MeV. Furthermore, they measured, for the first time, the absolute branching ratio of Ds​0∗​±→Ds±​π0→superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠0superscript2317plus-or-minussuperscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠plus-or-minussuperscript𝜋0Ds0*pmto Dspmpi0 and found it to be 1.00−0.14+0.00​(stat)−0.14+0.00​(syst)superscriptsubscript1.000.140.00(stat)superscript0.140.00(syst)1.00-0.14+0.00mbox(stat)-0.14+0.00mbox(syst)citebib.bib4. Initially, it was assumed that the Ds​0∗​superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠02317Ds0* meson is a conventional P𝑃P-wave c​s¯𝑐¯𝑠cbars state with JP=0+superscript𝐽𝑃superscript0JP=0+ citebib.bib5, bib.bib6. However, the predicted mass based on this assumption turned out to be approximately 150150150 MeV lower than the results from quark models and lattice calculations citebib.bib5, bib.bib6, bib.bib7, bib.bib8, bib.bib9, bib.bib10, bib.bib11. This discrepancy led to suggestions that the Ds​0∗​superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠02317Ds0* meson could be a non-Q​q¯𝑄¯𝑞Qbarq state, such as a molecular or tetraquark state citebib.bib12, bib.bib13, bib.bib14, bib.bib15, bib.bib16, bib.bib17, bib.bib18, bib.bib19, bib.bib20, bib.bib21, bib.bib22, bib.bib23, bib.bib24, bib.bib25. The Ds​0∗​superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠02317Ds0* lies in the proximity of the D​K𝐷𝐾DK threshold, being located at about 45 MeV below it. This suggests the possibility of considering it as a D​K𝐷𝐾DK molecular state. Furthermore, the decay width of the Ds​0∗​superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠02317Ds0* meson is observed to be extremely narrow, with only an upper limit known: ΓDs​π<3.8subscriptΓsubscript𝐷𝑠𝜋3.8GammaDspi<3.8 MeV at the 95%percent9595% confidence level citebib.bib26. This narrow decay width can be attributed to the strong isospin violation involved in the Ds​0∗​→Ds​π→superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠02317subscript𝐷𝑠𝜋Ds0*to Dspi decay. Several theoretical approaches have been proposed to explain the decay modes of Ds​0∗​superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠02317Ds0* citebib.bib27, bib.bib28, bib.bib21, bib.bib29, bib.bib25, bib.bib30, bib.bib31. Given that the Ds​0∗​superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠02317Ds0* is just below the D​K𝐷𝐾DK threshold, it is reasonable to investigate its nature as a dynamically generated D​K𝐷𝐾DK molecular state. Several theoretical works have provided strong support for this hypothesis. For instance, in Refs. citebib.bib16, bib.bib17, bib.bib18, bib.bib19, bib.bib20, bib.bib32, bib.bib33, it was shown through the coupled-channel formalism that the Ds​0∗​superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠02317Ds0* can indeed be dynamically generated. These works considered the Ds​ηsubscript𝐷𝑠𝜂Dseta channel, taking into account the π0−ηsuperscript𝜋0𝜂pi0-eta mixing. Lattice calculations have also considered the coupled-channel effects. For example, Mohler et al. citebib.bib24 considered both the c​s¯𝑐¯𝑠cbars and D​K𝐷𝐾DK interpolators, while in Refs. citebib.bib25, bib.bib30, bib.bib34, bib.bib35, bib.bib36 the D​K𝐷𝐾DK and Ds​ηsubscript𝐷𝑠𝜂Dseta channels were introduced. For a comprehensive review of the experimental and theoretical status, we refer to the work by Chen et al. citebib.bib37. In this study, we aim to demonstrate how the Ds​0∗​superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠02317Ds0* meson can be dynamically generated as a D​K𝐷𝐾DK molecular state using the coupled-channel formalism within the framework of the meson-exchange modelcitebib.bib38. Janssen et al. citebib.bib38 previously showed the dynamical generation of the f0​subscript𝑓0980f0 and a0​subscript𝑎0980a0 scalar mesons by constructing the π​π𝜋𝜋pipi, π​η𝜋𝜂pieta, and K​K¯𝐾¯𝐾KbarK coupled channels. Recently, a meson-exchange model with a fully off-shell coupled-channel formalism was further developed and successfully applied to describe the dynamically generated axial-vector mesons a1​subscript𝑎11260a1 and b1​subscript𝑏11235b1 in π​ρ𝜋𝜌pirho scatteringcitebib.bib39, bib.bib40. Considering that the f0​subscript𝑓0980f0 and a0​subscript𝑎0980a0 mesons lie below the K​K¯𝐾¯𝐾KbarK threshold and the masses of a1​subscript𝑎11260a1 and b1​subscript𝑏11235b1 are located below the K​K¯∗𝐾superscript¯𝐾KbarK* threshold, we expect that the Ds​0∗​superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠02317Ds0* meson can also be produced as a dynamically generated scalar meson located below the D​K𝐷𝐾DK threshold. Therefore, we will show in the current work that the Ds​0∗​superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠02317Ds0* meson emerges as a D​K𝐷𝐾DK molecular state within the framework of the meson-exchange model. To achieve this, we compute the kernel amplitudes using heavy effective Lagrangians satisfying heavy-quark symmetrycitebib.bib41, bib.bib42, bib.bib43, flavor SU symmetry, and hidden local symmetrycitebib.bib44, bib.bib45, bib.bib46. We then solve the fully off-shell coupled Blankenbecler-Sugar (BbS) integral equations derived from the Bethe-Salpeter equation with three-dimensional reductioncitebib.bib47, bib.bib48 with sixteen different channels considered. The structure of the present work is outlined as follows: In Section II, we provide an overview of the general formalism employed in the meson-exchange model with the coupled-channel formalism. In Section III, we demonstrate the emergence of the Ds​0∗​superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠02317Ds0* meson as a D​K𝐷𝐾DK molecular state and present the corresponding numerical results. Additionally, we extend our analysis to predict the existence of the Bs​0∗superscriptsubscript𝐵𝑠0Bs0* meson as a B​K𝐵𝐾BK molecular state. Finally, in the last section, we present our conclusions, summarizing the key findings and contributions of this study.

The heavy-quark spin-flavor symmetry allows one to treat the D𝐷D and B𝐵B mesons on an equal footing. Since no neutral Ds∗superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠Ds* state exists, the u𝑢u channel diagrams for the D​K→D​K→𝐷𝐾𝐷𝐾DKto DK amplitude are absent. We will discuss this effect later. The Ds+​π0superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscript𝜋0Ds+pi0 channel provides a background. The two D​K𝐷𝐾DK channels play key roles in generating the Ds​0∗​superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠02317Ds0* resonance, while the Ds+​ηsuperscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠𝜂Ds+eta channel shifts the pole position. We list the cutoff parameters in Table. The matrix elements of 𝒱𝒱V are depicted in Fig. . We observe that the transition amplitudes 𝒱Ds+​π0→D0​K+subscript𝒱→superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscript𝜋0superscript𝐷0superscript𝐾VDs+pi0to D0K+ and 𝒱Ds+​π0→D+​K0subscript𝒱→superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscript𝜋0superscript𝐷superscript𝐾0VDs+pi0to D+K0 dominate over all other contributions, primarily due to the large coupling constants in the D∗superscript𝐷D* exchange diagrams in the u𝑢u channel. The D​K→D​K→𝐷𝐾𝐷𝐾DKto DK amplitudes provide significant contributions because of the large value of gD​D∗​Psubscript𝑔𝐷superscript𝐷𝑃gDD*P in the u𝑢u channel. The Ds+​η→D​K→superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠𝜂𝐷𝐾Ds+etato DK amplitude contains the SU symmetric factor of 1/6161/sqrt6, which is smaller than that of 1/2121/sqrt2 for the Ds+​π0superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscript𝜋0Ds+pi0 channel. Consequently, it gives a smaller contribution to the T𝑇T-matrix. In the case of the D0​K+→D+​K0→superscript𝐷0superscript𝐾superscript𝐷superscript𝐾0D0K+to D+K0 amplitudes, no u𝑢u channel process is allowed due to the absence of the neutral Ds∗superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠Ds*. As a result, its contribution is expected to be marginal. We are now in a position to evaluate the coupled integral equations. content_sections.III Results and Discussion: We first examine the transition amplitudes TJ=0superscript𝑇𝐽0TJ=0 in the complex plane to see how the poles appear. To analyze the coupled-channel effects, we strictly follow our cutoff scheme Λ=mex+600Λsubscript𝑚ex600Lambda=mex+600 MeV for a while. Note that the kernel amplitudes do not contain any singularities since we do not include s𝑠s channel diagrams. By solving the two-body integral equation with a single channel, we can identify which channels give rise to resonant states. Each T𝑇T-matrix element is depicted in the left panel of Fig. . We see that the strong attraction in the u𝑢u-channel exchange generates two poles in the D0​K+superscript𝐷0superscript𝐾D0K+ and D+​K0superscript𝐷superscript𝐾0D+K0 channels. Though the kernel amplitudes for the Ds+​π0→D​K→superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscript𝜋0𝐷𝐾Ds+pi0to DK and and Ds+​η→D​K→superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠𝜂𝐷𝐾Ds+etato DK transitions also incorporate the u𝑢u-channel diagrams, no poles appear in the corresponding T𝑇T-matrix. Nevertheless, the Ds+​π0→D0​K+→superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscript𝜋0superscript𝐷0superscript𝐾Ds+pi0to D0K+ and Ds+​π0→D+​K0→superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscript𝜋0superscript𝐷superscript𝐾0Ds+pi0to D+K0 contributions show a significant enhacement at the Ds+​π0superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscript𝜋0Ds+pi0 threshold energy. No pole structures were found in other T𝑇T matrix elements. We multiply the coupling strength gD​D∗​Msubscript𝑔𝐷superscript𝐷𝑀gDD*M by a control parameter b𝑏b to see the effects. In the right panel of Fig. , we depict the u𝑢u-channel dependence of the TD0​K+→D0​K+J=0superscriptsubscript𝑇→superscript𝐷0superscript𝐾superscript𝐷0superscript𝐾𝐽0TD0K+to D0K+J=0 with b𝑏b varied from b=0.7𝑏0.7b=0.7 to b=1.2𝑏1.2b=1.2. Note that the pole, which arises from the strong attraction in the D0​K+superscript𝐷0superscript𝐾D0K+ kernel amplitude, appears only if the value of b𝑏b is approximately greater than 0.7. As anticipated, the energy corresponding to this pole increases as b𝑏b becomes larger. We now consider the S-wave transition amplitude for the D0​K+→D0​K+→superscript𝐷0superscript𝐾superscript𝐷0superscript𝐾D0K+to D0K+ process. When the D0​K+superscript𝐷0superscript𝐾D0K+ channel is coupled to the D+​K0superscript𝐷superscript𝐾0D+K0 channel, the two poles still remain below the D0​K+superscript𝐷0superscript𝐾D0K+ threshold, as depicted in Fig. . However, once we couple it to one of the non-diagonal amplitudes, only the first pole starts to undergo changes. For example, let us couple the Ds+​π0superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscript𝜋0Ds+pi0 amplitude to the D0​K+→D+​K0→superscript𝐷0superscript𝐾superscript𝐷superscript𝐾0D0K+to D+K0 transition. We can scrutinize the coupled-channel effects by varying the coupling strength of the two channels, which is controlled by parameter b𝑏b. As b𝑏b increases, the width of the first pole is rapidly broadened, as shown in Fig. . By setting b=1𝑏1b=1, which corresponds to our final results, the first pole completely disappears. Consequently, we observe only the second pole that remains largely unaffected by the Ds+​π0superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscript𝜋0Ds+pi0 channel. Next, we discuss how the Ds+​ηsuperscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠𝜂Ds+eta channel comes into play. If we couple Ds+​π0superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscript𝜋0Ds+pi0, D​K𝐷𝐾DK, and Ds+​ηsuperscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠𝜂Ds+eta channels together, the second pole starts to move to the lower energy, which is demonstrated in Fig. . When we introduce all possible coupled channels, the pole is finally positioned at approximately sR=(2261.4−i​0.072)subscript𝑠𝑅2261.4𝑖0.072sqrtsR=(2261.4-i0.072) MeV. The pole position on the real axis Re​sR=2261.4Resubscript𝑠𝑅2261.4Re,sqrtsR=2261.4 MeV is still deviated from the experimental data for the Ds​0∗​superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠02317Ds0* mass. Thus, we need to fit the model parameters, keeping their uncertainties as low as possible. We can itemize how we can proceed to fit the Ds​0∗​superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠02317Ds0* mass: • We can slightly change the cutoff masses listed in Table. • We still have one more free parameter, i. e. , gη​η​σsubscript𝑔𝜂𝜂𝜎getaetasigma. Fortunately, the effect of gη​η​σsubscript𝑔𝜂𝜂𝜎getaetasigma is almost negligible. • We can also consider the effects of the explicit flavor SU symmetry breaking by about 101010, and find that they are also negligible. So, we concentrate on the fitting of the cutoff masses in such a way that the corresponding values are not much deviated from our recipe Λ≃mex+600similar-to-or-equalsΛsubscript𝑚ex600Lambdasimeq mex+600 MeV. As listed in Table, the values of the cutoff masses for the exchanged light mesons are kept to be Λ=mex+600Λsubscript𝑚ex600Lambda=mex+600 MeV, whereas we choose slightly smaller values for the exchanged heavy mesons Λ≈mex+540Λsubscript𝑚ex540Lambdaapprox mex+540 MeV. Finally, we arrive at the physical pole position in the second Riemann sheet: sR=(2317.90−i​0.0593)subscript𝑠𝑅2317.90𝑖0.0593sqrtsR=(2317.90-i0.0593) MeV as shown in Fig. , where we plot the pole corresponding to the Ds​0∗​superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠02317Ds0* meson in the complex plane. The pole for the Ds​0∗​superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠02317Ds0* meson is located approximately 40 MeV below the D​K𝐷𝐾DK thresholds, which indicates that the Ds​0∗​superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠02317Ds0* may be identified as a D​K𝐷𝐾DK molecular statecitebib.bib17, bib.bib18, bib.bib20. To explore the effects of the coupled channels in more detail, we analyze the channel couplings for the channels involved. The transition amplitudes in the vicinity of the pole position sRsubscript𝑠𝑅sqrtsR can be parameterized as Ta​b​(s)=4​π​ga​gbs−sR. subscript𝑇𝑎𝑏𝑠4𝜋subscript𝑔𝑎subscript𝑔𝑏𝑠subscript𝑠𝑅displaystyle Tab(sqrts)=4pifracgagbs-sR. The residue can be extracted by taking the limit s→sR→𝑠subscript𝑠𝑅sto sR as follows: ℛa​b=lims→sR(sR−s)​Ta​b/(4​π). subscriptℛ𝑎𝑏subscript→𝑠subscript𝑠𝑅subscript𝑠𝑅𝑠subscript𝑇𝑎𝑏4𝜋displaystyleRab=limsto sR(sR-s)Tab/(4pi). The channel coupling is defined by ga=ℛa​a,subscript𝑔𝑎subscriptℛ𝑎𝑎displaystyle ga=sqrtRaa, which characterizes the coupling strength of the corresponding resonance with a given channel a𝑎a. We predict the four different channel couplings as follows: gDs+​π0=0.00967+i​0.05294​GeV,gDs+​η=−85.25−i​0.0610​GeV,formulae-sequencesubscript𝑔superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscript𝜋00.00967𝑖0.05294GeVsubscript𝑔superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠𝜂85.25𝑖0.0610GeVdisplaystyle gDs+pi0=0.00967+i0.05294,GeV,;;;gDs+eta=-85.25-i0.0610,GeV, gD0​K+=−77.59+i​0.730​GeV,gD+​K0=−80.16−i​0.905​GeV. formulae-sequencesubscript𝑔superscript𝐷0superscript𝐾77.59𝑖0.730GeVsubscript𝑔superscript𝐷superscript𝐾080.16𝑖0.905GeVdisplaystyle gD0K+=-77.59+i0.730,GeV,;;;gD+K0=-80.16-i0.905,GeV. We can also define the corresponding coupling strengths: g1=|gDs+​π0|=5.381×10−2​GeV,subscript𝑔1subscript𝑔superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscript𝜋05.381superscript102GeVdisplaystyle g1=|gDs+pi0|=5.381times 10-2,GeV, g2=|gD0​K+|=77.59​GeV,subscript𝑔2subscript𝑔superscript𝐷0superscript𝐾77.59GeVdisplaystyle g2=|gD0K+|=77.59,GeV, g3=|gD+​K0|=80.17​GeV,subscript𝑔3subscript𝑔superscript𝐷superscript𝐾080.17GeVdisplaystyle g3=|gD+K0|=80.17,GeV, g4=|gDs+​η|=85.25​GeV. subscript𝑔4subscript𝑔superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠𝜂85.25GeVdisplaystyle g4=|gDs+eta|=85.25,GeV. Note that the channels corresponding to gisubscript𝑔𝑖gi (i=2, 3, 4𝑖234i=2,,3,,4) are not experimentally reachable by kinematics. The value of g1subscript𝑔1g1 is the predicted one, since all the parameters are fixed by reproducing the mass of the Ds​0∗​superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠02317Ds0* meson. Using the predicted value of g1subscript𝑔1g1, we determine the Ds​0∗→Ds+​π0→superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠0superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscript𝜋0Ds0*to Ds+pi0 decay width: ΓDs​0∗→Ds+​π0=g12mDs​0∗​ρDs+​π0​(mDs​0∗2)=g12mDs​0∗2​pcm4​π=13.86​keV,subscriptΓ→superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠0superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscript𝜋0superscriptsubscript𝑔12subscript𝑚superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠0subscript𝜌superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscript𝜋0superscriptsubscript𝑚superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠02superscriptsubscript𝑔12superscriptsubscript𝑚superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠02subscript𝑝cm4𝜋13.86keVdisplaystyleGammaDs0*to Ds+pi0=fracg12mDs0*rhoDs+pi0(mDs0*2)=fracg12mDs0*2fracpcm4pi=13.86,keV, where ρR​(s)subscript𝜌𝑅𝑠rhoR(s) stands for the two-body phase space factor and pcmsubscript𝑝cmpcm denotes the center of momentum for the final states. The predicted value ΓDs​0∗→Ds+​π0=13.86​keVsubscriptΓ→superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠0superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscript𝜋013.86keVGammaDs0*to Ds+pi0=13.86,keV lies within the experimental upper limit of 3.83.83.8 MeV. The coupling strengths convey information on which channel is the strongest one. We conclude from the results in Eq. that the D​K𝐷𝐾DK channel is the most dominant one, and the Ds+​ηsuperscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠𝜂Ds+eta is the second most significant one. In Table, we compare the current results for the channel couplings with those from other works. Interestingly, The values of the D​K𝐷𝐾DK and Ds+​ηsuperscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠𝜂Ds+eta coupling strengths from the present work are much larger than those from other works. Within the same theoretical framework, we extend our analysis to the scalar bottom-strange meson. Specifically, we apply the coupled-channel formalism to investigate the B¯​ℳ¯𝐵ℳbarBM interaction with positive strangeness +11+1. It is of great importance to note that there is a distinction compared to the charm sector, as the u𝑢u-channel process in the B−​K+superscript𝐵superscript𝐾B-K+ channel is not allowed due to the absence of the charged Bs∗superscriptsubscript𝐵𝑠Bs* meson. The cutoff parameters, ΛB∗=630subscriptΛsuperscript𝐵630LambdaB*=630 MeV and ΛBs∗=390subscriptΛsuperscriptsubscript𝐵𝑠390LambdaBs*=390 MeV, are used for the u𝑢u-channel diagrams. The pole for the Bs​0∗superscriptsubscript𝐵𝑠0Bs0* emerges again in the second Riemann sheet, located at sR=(5756.32−i​0.0228)subscript𝑠𝑅5756.32𝑖0.0228sqrtsR=(5756.32-i0.0228) MeV, as depicted in Fig. . Notably, the total width for the Bs​0∗superscriptsubscript𝐵𝑠0Bs0* resonance, ΓB¯s​0∗subscriptΓsuperscriptsubscript¯𝐵𝑠0GammabarBs0*, exhibits a comparable value to that of the Ds​0∗superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠0Ds0* resonance. This observation is understandable when considering that the two-body phase space factor, ρB¯s0​π0​(mB¯s​0∗2)subscript𝜌superscriptsubscript¯𝐵𝑠0superscript𝜋0superscriptsubscript𝑚superscriptsubscript¯𝐵𝑠02rhobarBs0pi0(mbarBs0*2) for the B¯s​0∗superscriptsubscript¯𝐵𝑠0barBs0* resonance is approximately two times smaller than that of the Ds​0∗superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠0Ds0*. We exmaine the channel couplings for the bottom-strange meson as follows: gB¯s0​π0=0.104−i​0.139​GeV,gB¯s0​η=−302.0−i​1.491​GeV,formulae-sequencesubscript𝑔superscriptsubscript¯𝐵𝑠0superscript𝜋00.104𝑖0.139GeVsubscript𝑔superscriptsubscript¯𝐵𝑠0𝜂302.0𝑖1.491GeVdisplaystyle gbarBs0pi0=0.104-i0.139,GeV,;;;gbarBs0eta=-302.0-i1.491,GeV, gB−​K+=−396.8+i​0.318​GeV,gB¯0​K0=−134.07+i​5.340​GeV. formulae-sequencesubscript𝑔superscript𝐵superscript𝐾396.8𝑖0.318GeVsubscript𝑔superscript¯𝐵0superscript𝐾0134.07𝑖5.340GeVdisplaystyle gB-K+=-396.8+i0.318,GeV,;;;gbarB0K034.07+i5.340,GeV. The corresponding coupling strengths are given by g1=|gB¯s0​π0|=0.174​GeV,subscript𝑔1subscript𝑔superscriptsubscript¯𝐵𝑠0superscript𝜋00.174GeVdisplaystyle g1=|gbarBs0pi0|=0.174,GeV, g2=|gB−​K+|=302.03​GeV,subscript𝑔2subscript𝑔superscript𝐵superscript𝐾302.03GeVdisplaystyle g2=|gB-K+|=302.03,GeV, g3=|gB¯0​K0|=396.75​GeV,subscript𝑔3subscript𝑔superscript¯𝐵0superscript𝐾0396.75GeVdisplaystyle g3=|gbarB0K0|=396.75,GeV, g4=|gB¯s0​η|=134.18​GeV. subscript𝑔4subscript𝑔superscriptsubscript¯𝐵𝑠0𝜂134.18GeVdisplaystyle g4=|gbarBs0eta|=134.18,GeV. In the bottom sector, the B​K𝐵𝐾BK channel is the most dominant one.
content_sections.IV Summary and conclusions: In the current work, we aimed to investigate the Ds​0∗​superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠02317Ds0*, studying Ds+​π0superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscript𝜋0Ds+pi0 scattering within the off-shell coupled channel formalism. We introduced sixteen different coupled channels in the charge basis: Ds+​π0superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscript𝜋0Ds+pi0, D0​K+superscript𝐷0superscript𝐾D0K+, D+​K0superscript𝐷superscript𝐾0D+K0 and Ds+​ηsuperscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠𝜂Ds+eta with positive strangeness +11+1. We first constructed the invariant kernel amplitudes based on the effective Lagrangians derived from heavy-quark effective theory and hidden local symmetry. Having inserted the kernel amplitudes into the Blankenbecler-Sugar-type coupled integral equations, we evaluated the transition amplitudes. Since the Ds​0∗​superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠02317Ds0* is the scalar meson, we carried out the partial-wave expansion to pick out the S-wave transition amplitude. We then scrutinized the D​K𝐷𝐾DK channels how the pole appears dynamically. When we only considered the D​K𝐷𝐾DK channels, two poles emerged. Adding different channels one by one, the width of the first pole became broadened. Once we included all the coupled channels, the first pole disappeared, and at the same time the position of the second pole started to move to lower energies. Having fitted the cutoff masses, the pole is positioned at the physical mass of the Ds​0∗​superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠02317Ds0*. The Ds​0∗→Ds+​π0→superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠0superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscript𝜋0Ds0*to Ds+pi0 decay width was predicted to be 13.8613.8613.86 keV, which lies within the experimental upper limit. The D​K𝐷𝐾DK channels were found to be the most dominant one. In addition, we predicted the mass and width of the Bs​0∗superscriptsubscript𝐵𝑠0Bs0* meson. Both the Ds​0∗superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠0Ds0* and Bs​0∗superscriptsubscript𝐵𝑠0Bs0* are positioned below the D​K𝐷𝐾DK and B¯​K¯𝐵𝐾barBK thresholds, respectively. It indicates that the Ds​0∗superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠0Ds0* and Bs​0∗superscriptsubscript𝐵𝑠0Bs0* mesons can be interpreted as the D​K𝐷𝐾DK and B¯​K¯𝐵𝐾barBK molecular states. Acknowledgements. We are very grateful to Samson Clymton for useful comments and discussion. The present work was supported by Basic Science Research Program through the National Research Foundation of Korea funded by the Korean government (Ministry of Education, Science and Technology, MEST), Grant-No. 2021R1A2C2093368 and 2018R1A5A1025563.

all_papers.title:
Estimates of the isospin-violating $\Lambda_b\rightarrow \Sigma^0 \phi,
  \Sigma^0 J/\psi$ decays and the $\Sigma-\Lambda$ mixing
all_papers.abstract:
We analyse the two purely isospin-violating decays $\Lambda_b\rightarrow
\Sigma^0 \phi$ and $\Lambda_b\rightarrow \Sigma^0 J/\psi$, proceed merely via
the exchange topologies, in the framework of perturbative QCD approach.
Assuming $\Sigma^0$ baryon belongs to the idealized isospin triplet with quark
components of $usd$, the branching ratios of the two decay modes are predicted
to be tiny, of the order $10^{-8}-10^{-9}$, leading to a difficulty in
observing them. We then extend our study to include the $\Sigma-\Lambda$
mixing.It is found that the mixing has significant effect on the
$\Lambda_b\rightarrow \Sigma$ decays, especially it can greatly increase the
rate of the $J/\psi$ process, by as much as two orders of magnitude, yield
$10^{-7}$, which should be searchable in the future. We also estimate a set of
asymmetry observables with and without the mixing effect, which will be tested
in coming experiments.

sections.3: Fleischer, Search for the angle gamma in the electroweak penguin dominated decay Bs→π0​Φ→subscript𝐵𝑠superscript𝜋0ΦB_{s}\to\pi^{0}\Phi, Phys

qualitative: Isospin symmetry is an approximate symmetry in the Standard Model, used to relate amplitudes of decays involving up and down quarks; it is violated by the mass difference between down and up quarks and electromagnetic corrections.

The Λb→Σ⁰ϕ and Λb→Σ⁰J/ψ decays are purely isospin-violating because the Λb is an isospin singlet while the final states have total isospin I=1, leading to no contribution from isospin-conserving QCD penguin operators.

These decays proceed exclusively via tree-level and electroweak penguin operators, which are isospin-violating, making them sensitive probes of electroweak penguin contributions and potential new physics.
The Σ⁰ baryon is assumed to belong to an idealized isospin triplet with quark content usd, but in reality, it mixes with the Λ⁰ due to strong isospin symmetry breaking, known as Σ−Λ mixing.

The Σ−Λ mixing significantly enhances the decay rate of Λb→Σ⁰J/ψ by up to two orders of magnitude, increasing the branching ratio to around 10⁻⁷, which is potentially observable in future experiments.

The perturbative QCD (PQCD) approach is suitable for analyzing these decays because it can consistently include non-factorizable exchange topologies, unlike factorization approaches or diquark approximations which fail due to orthogonality of Λb and Σ⁰ spin wave functions.
The PQCD framework uses light-cone distribution amplitudes (LCDAs) and Sudakov suppression to handle large logarithms and provide convergent, predictive results for exclusive baryonic decays.
Asymmetry observables are estimated both with and without Σ−Λ mixing, providing testable predictions for future experimental measurements at LHCb and future B factories.
quantitative: Branching ratios for Λb→Σ⁰ϕ and Λb→Σ⁰J/ψ are predicted to be of order 10⁻⁸–10⁻⁹ under the idealized isospin triplet assumption (no Σ−Λ mixing).
Inclusion of Σ−Λ mixing increases the branching ratio of Λb→Σ⁰J/ψ by up to two orders of magnitude, reaching approximately 10⁻⁷.

The LHCb observation of Bs⁰→ϕρ⁰ has a branching fraction of (2.7±0.8)×10⁻⁷, which is consistent with SM expectations and leaves little room for new physics, motivating the study of other isospin-violating decays.
The Σ−Λ mixing effect is significant in the Λb→Σ⁰J/ψ decay, but less so in the Λb→Σ⁰ϕ decay, indicating a stronger sensitivity of the J/ψ channel to mixing.
The PQCD approach allows for a self-consistent evaluation of all Feynman diagrams, including exchange topologies, without relying on factorization assumptions.
