论文全面覆盖SU(3)对称性与U-spin、同位旋的理论框架，明确研究重味分子态的SU(3)极限与破缺，提出广义味-自旋对称性，与查询高度契合。  

约束项检查：  
- C1：研究SU(3)味对称性 — 满足。证据：“We predict the possible $D^{(*)}D^{(*)}_s$ molecular states in the SU(3) limit” 对比 “SU(3) flavor symmetry”  
- C2：研究U-spin与同位旋 — 满足。证据：“generalize our isospin criterion ... to the $U/V$ spin case” 对比 “U-spin, isospin”  

不相关性 / 风险点 / 缺口：  
- 无。论文直接回应查询中所有核心概念，无偏离。
authors: Kan Chen, Bo Wang, and Shi-Lin Zhu

content_sections.I Introduction: The study of exotic hadrons with configurations beyond the conventional quark model has become a hot topic since the discovery of X​𝑋3872X citebib.bib1. Especially, the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc citebib.bib2, bib.bib3, Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs citebib.bib4 together with the recently reported Tc​csubscript𝑇𝑐𝑐Tcc citebib.bib5, bib.bib6 are manifestly exotic states. The observation of these states indicates that the interactions between heavy flavor di-hadrons share very similar binding mechanisms. Since the observed Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc, Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs, and Tc​csubscript𝑇𝑐𝑐Tcc are below the thresholds of the corresponding di-hadron systems from several to several tens MeVs, a natural explanation is that these states are molecules. To describe the possible similarities of the binding mechanisms between the above heavy flavor di-hadron systems, we proposed a hydrogen-like picture and studied the heavy flavor meson-meson (M𝑀M–M𝑀M), baryon-meson (B𝐵B–M𝑀M), and baryon-baryon (B𝐵B–B𝐵B) molecular states in Ref. citebib.bib7. We assume that the interactions of heavy flavor di-hadron systems are mainly from the exchange of the light flavor mesons, which is closely related to the flavor and spin structures of light degrees of freedom (d. o. f) in the di-hadron systems. To address the above molecule picture, we adopt a quark-level Lagrangian citebib.bib8, bib.bib9, bib.bib10, bib.bib7 to describe the interactions of different heavy flavor di-hadron systems. Then the corresponding effective potentials can be parameterized in terms of the light quark-quark coupling constants and the flavor and spin matrix elements which are related to its light d. o. f. In this framework, we obtain a satisfactory description of the Tc​csubscript𝑇𝑐𝑐Tcc, Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc, Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states, and other possible bound states in the heavy flavor meson-meson, baryon-meson, and baryon-baryon di-hadron systems which arise from the isospin singlet and triplet meson exchange interactions. The interactions of different heavy flavor di-hadron systems (M𝑀M–M𝑀M, B𝐵B–M𝑀M, and B𝐵B–B𝐵B) can be understood in a molecule picture simultaneously. The simple Lagrangian introduced in our work possesses two important advantages in Ref. citebib.bib7. It not only relates the interactions of different heavy flavor di-hadron (M𝑀M–M𝑀M, B𝐵B–M𝑀M, and B𝐵B–B𝐵B) systems via their flavor-spin structures but also relates the interactions of the multiplets in a specific heavy flavor di-hadron system. In Table , we list the systems which were already studied in Ref. citebib.bib7. In this work, we further adopt this framework to study the rest of the doubly heavy flavor di-hadron systems. The inclusion of the rest of the doubly heavy flavor di-hadron systems allows us to present a full analysis of the possible flavor-spin symmetry and the interactions of molecular multiplets in a specific di-hadron systems. This is the main task of this work. Some of the doubly heavy tetraquark states with strangeness have already been discussed in literature. For example, the strange doubly heavy tetraquark states in molecular configurations have been discussed in Refs. citebib.bib16, bib.bib11, bib.bib12, bib.bib13, bib.bib14, bib.bib15, bib.bib17. The compact strange tetraquark states were studied via various methods, such as quark potential models citebib.bib18, bib.bib19, bib.bib20, bib.bib21, lattice QCD citebib.bib22, chromo-magnetic interaction model (CMI) citebib.bib23, bib.bib24, bib.bib25, bib.bib26, and QCD sum rule citebib.bib27, bib.bib28. The strange doubly heavy baryon-meson and di-baryon systems have also been discussed in literature. The possible pentaquark states composed of q​q​q​c​c¯𝑞𝑞𝑞𝑐¯𝑐qqqcbarc configurations with q=u𝑞𝑢q=u, d𝑑d, and s𝑠s were studied within the framework of CMI model citebib.bib29. The OBE model was adopted to study the interactions of the Ξc(′,∗)​D¯s(∗)Xic(prime,*)barDs(*) citebib.bib30 and Ωc(∗)​D¯s(∗)superscriptsubscriptΩ𝑐superscriptsubscript¯𝐷𝑠Omegac(*)barDs(*) citebib.bib31 systems. In Ref. citebib.bib32, the hidden charm pentaquark states composed of q​q​s​c​c¯𝑞𝑞𝑠𝑐¯𝑐qqscbarc, q​s​s​c​c¯𝑞𝑠𝑠𝑐¯𝑐qsscbarc, and s​s​s​c​c¯𝑠𝑠𝑠𝑐¯𝑐ssscbarc configurations are studied in a diquark-diquark-antiquark model. A general discussion of the heavy-antiheavy hadronic molecules was performed in Ref. citebib.bib33. In addition, the authors in Ref. citebib.bib34 adopted the OBE model to discuss the di-baryon systems composed of spin-12/321232frac12/frac32 and flavor-𝟑¯/𝟔bold-¯36bar3/6 single heavy baryons systematically. A general discussion on these systems based on the resonance saturation model can be found in Ref. citebib.bib16. This paper is organized as follows. In Sec. , we introduce our theoretical framework. Then we present our numerical results and discussions in Sec. . In Sec. , we conclude this work with a short summary.
content_sections.II Theoretical framework: The interactions for the heavy flavor di-hadron systems listed in Table can be calculated by considering the possible contributions from the exchanges of light mesons, which depend on the flavor and spin structures of the heavy flavor di-hadron systems from their light d. o. f. On the other hand, the S𝑆S-wave interactions of the ground heavy flavor di-hadron systems may arise from the exchange of the JP=0+superscript𝐽𝑃superscript0JP=0+ scalar or 1+superscript11+ axial vector mesons. Thus, we introduce the following quark level Lagrangians citebib.bib8, bib.bib9, bib.bib10, bib.bib7 ℒ=gs​q¯​𝒮​q+ga​q¯​γμ​γ5​𝒜μ​qℒsubscript𝑔𝑠¯𝑞𝒮𝑞subscript𝑔𝑎¯𝑞subscript𝛾𝜇superscript𝛾5superscript𝒜𝜇𝑞displaystyleL=gsbarqSq+gabarqgammamugamma5Amuq to describe the interactions among the considered ground heavy flavor di-hadron systems. Here, q=(u,d,s)𝑞𝑢𝑑𝑠q=(u,d,s), gssubscript𝑔𝑠gs and gasubscript𝑔𝑎ga are two independent coupling constants that encode the nonperturbative low energy dynamics of the di-hadron systems. From Eq. , the effective potential of the light quark-quark interactions reads Vq​qsubscript𝑉𝑞𝑞displaystyle Vqq =displaystyle= gs​𝝀1⋅𝝀2+ga​𝝀1⋅𝝀2​𝝈1⋅𝝈2⋅subscript𝑔𝑠subscript𝝀1subscript𝝀2⋅⋅subscript𝑔𝑎subscript𝝀1subscript𝝀2subscript𝝈1subscript𝝈2displaystyletildegslambda1cdotlambda2+tildegalambda1cdotlambda2sigma1cdotsigma2 Here, i𝑖i and j𝑗j sum from 1 to 3 and 4 to 7, respectively. The λ18​λ28superscriptsubscript𝜆18superscriptsubscript𝜆28lambda18lambda28, λ1i​λ2isuperscriptsubscript𝜆1𝑖superscriptsubscript𝜆2𝑖lambda1ilambda2i, and λ1j​λ2jsuperscriptsubscript𝜆1𝑗superscriptsubscript𝜆2𝑗lambda1jlambda2j are the SU flavor matrices of the isospin singlet, triplet, and two doublets, respectively. The 𝝈1⋅𝝈2⋅subscript𝝈1subscript𝝈2sigma1cdotsigma2 is the Pauli matrix in spin space. The redefined coupling constants are gs=gs2/m𝒮2subscript𝑔𝑠superscriptsubscript𝑔𝑠2superscriptsubscript𝑚𝒮2tildegs=gs2/mS2 and ga=ga2/m𝒜2subscript𝑔𝑎superscriptsubscript𝑔𝑎2subscriptsuperscript𝑚2𝒜tildega=ga2/m2A. The local form of the effective potential Vq​qsubscript𝑉𝑞𝑞Vqq is the result of integrating out the exchanged spurions. The effective potential in Eq. consists of three sets of flavor related operators and three sets of flavor-spin related operators. For convenience, we adopt the following notations 𝒪1subscript𝒪1displaystyleO1 =displaystyle= λ18​λ28,𝒪4=λ18​λ28​𝝈1⋅𝝈2,superscriptsubscript𝜆18superscriptsubscript𝜆28subscript𝒪4⋅superscriptsubscript𝜆18superscriptsubscript𝜆28subscript𝝈1subscript𝝈2displaystylelambda18lambda28,quadO4=lambda18lambda28sigma1cdotsigma2, 𝒪2subscript𝒪2displaystyleO2 =displaystyle= λ1i​λ2i,𝒪5=λ1i​λ2i​𝝈1⋅𝝈2,superscriptsubscript𝜆1𝑖superscriptsubscript𝜆2𝑖subscript𝒪5⋅superscriptsubscript𝜆1𝑖superscriptsubscript𝜆2𝑖subscript𝝈1subscript𝝈2displaystylelambda1ilambda2i,quadO5=lambda1ilambda2isigma1cdotsigma2, 𝒪3subscript𝒪3displaystyleO3 =displaystyle= λ1j​λ2j,𝒪6=λ1j​λ2j​𝝈1⋅𝝈2. superscriptsubscript𝜆1𝑗superscriptsubscript𝜆2𝑗subscript𝒪6⋅superscriptsubscript𝜆1𝑗superscriptsubscript𝜆2𝑗subscript𝝈1subscript𝝈2displaystylelambda1jlambda2j,quadO6=lambda1jlambda2jsigma1cdotsigma2. In our previous work citebib.bib7, we only consider the di-hadron systems without the strange meson exchange contribution. The coupling constants gssubscript𝑔𝑠tildegs and gasubscript𝑔𝑎tildega are obtained from a solvable Lippmann-Schwinger equation (LSE). For the heavy flavor di-hadron systems containing strange quark(s), the contributions from the λ1j​λ2jsuperscriptsubscript𝜆1𝑗superscriptsubscript𝜆2𝑗lambda1jlambda2j (j=4,5,6,7𝑗4567j=4,5,6,7) operator should also be included. Note that in the scalar or axial-vector SU flavor octet, the physical masses of the exchanged mesons that belong to the flavor isospin doublets are close to the masses of their isospin singlet or triplet mesons. Thus, we take the SU limit and set the coupling parameters gssubscript𝑔𝑠tildegs and gasubscript𝑔𝑎tildega to be equal to those of the isospin singlet or triplet mesons. The effective potential for a specific heavy flavor di-hadron H1​H2subscript𝐻1subscript𝐻2H1H2 system with total isospin I𝐼I and total angular momentum J𝐽J can be written as V[H1​H2]JI=⟨[H1​H2]JI|Vq​q|[H1​H2]JI⟩,subscript𝑉superscriptsubscriptdelimited-subscript𝐻1subscript𝐻2𝐽𝐼quantum-operator-productsuperscriptsubscriptdelimited-subscript𝐻1subscript𝐻2𝐽𝐼subscript𝑉𝑞𝑞superscriptsubscriptdelimited-subscript𝐻1subscript𝐻2𝐽𝐼displaystyle V[H1H2]JI=[H1H2]JI|Vqq|[H1H2]JI, where |[H1​H2]JI⟩ketsuperscriptsubscriptdelimited-subscript𝐻1subscript𝐻2𝐽𝐼|[H1H2]JI denotes the quark-level spin-flavor wave function of the H1​H2subscript𝐻1subscript𝐻2H1H2 system. Note that as an approximation, the correction from heavy degree of freedom is omitted. The operators 𝝀𝟏⋅𝝀𝟐⋅subscript𝝀1subscript𝝀2lambda1cdotlambda2 and 𝝈1⋅𝝈2⋅subscript𝝈1subscript𝝈2sigma1cdotsigma2 only act on the flavor and spin wave functions of the light quark components. As can be seen from Eq. , the interactions of the considered di-hadron systems only depend on the flavor (⟨𝝀1⋅𝝀2⟩delimited-⟨⟩⋅subscript𝝀1subscript𝝀2lambda1cdotlambda2) and spin (⟨𝝈1⋅𝝈2⟩delimited-⟨⟩⋅subscript𝝈1subscript𝝈2sigma1cdotsigma2) matrix elements, implying a flavor-spin symmetry for the interactions of the heavy flavor di-hadron systems. In the following, we will further discuss the properties of this symmetry.

At hadron level, the matrix elements of the 𝒍1⋅𝒍2⋅subscript𝒍1subscript𝒍2l1cdotl2 spin-spin operator in the light d. o. f for the discussed M𝑀M–M𝑀M, B𝐵B–M𝑀M, and B𝐵B–B𝐵B systems can be obtained via a spin rearrangement procedure. The di-hadron systems can be related to the states with a specific total light spin and a heavy spin by the relations citebib.bib35 |l1h1displaystyleleft|l1h1right. S1l2h2S2JM⟩displaystyleleft. S1l2h2S2JMright where lisubscript𝑙𝑖li and hisubscriptℎ𝑖hi are the light and heavy spin for Hisubscript𝐻𝑖Hi hadron, respectively. Then the matrix elements of the operator 𝒍1⋅𝒍2⋅subscript𝒍1subscript𝒍2l1cdotl2 can be obtained as ⟨𝒍1⋅𝒍2⟩delimited-⟨⟩⋅subscript𝒍1subscript𝒍2displaystyleleftl1cdotl2right =displaystyle= ∑L,H[L​(L+1)−l1​(l1+1)−l2​(l2+1)]subscript𝐿𝐻delimited-𝐿𝐿1subscript𝑙1subscript𝑙11subscript𝑙2subscript𝑙21displaystylesumL,Hleft[Lleft(L+1right)-l1left(l1+1right)-l2left(l2+1right)right] Since the hisubscriptℎ𝑖hi is identical for all the single heavy flavor hadrons, we use a more brief notation |l1,S1;l2,S2;J⟩ketsubscript𝑙1subscript𝑆1subscript𝑙2subscript𝑆2𝐽|l1,S1;l2,S2;J to specify the spin wave function of a [H1​H2]Jsubscriptdelimited-subscript𝐻1subscript𝐻2𝐽[H1H2]J di-hadron system. In Table , we collect the results of ⟨𝒍1⋅𝒍2⟩delimited-⟨⟩⋅subscript𝒍1subscript𝒍2l1cdotl2 matrix elements for the discussed M𝑀M–M𝑀M, B𝐵B–M𝑀M, and B𝐵B–B𝐵B di-hadron systems.
content_sections.II Theoretical framework II.2 The flavor: Now we discuss the quark-level flavor wave functions of the considered di-hadron systems. In the SU limit, the two anti-charmed mesons belong to the flavor 𝟑33 representation and have 𝟑⊗𝟑=𝟔⊕𝟑¯. tensor-product33direct-sum6¯3displaystyle3otimes3=6oplusbar3. Similarly, a charmed baryon and an anti-charmed meson can form the following representations (𝟑⊗𝟑)⊗𝟑tensor-producttensor-product333displaystyleleft(3otimes3right)otimes3 =displaystyle= (𝟔⊗𝟑)⊕(𝟑¯⊗𝟑)direct-sumtensor-product63tensor-product¯33displaystyleleft(6otimes3right)oplusleft(bar3otimes3right) Two singly charmed baryons have four light quarks. The product of four 𝟑33 representations can be divided into (𝟑⊗𝟑)⊗(𝟑⊗𝟑)tensor-producttensor-product33tensor-product33displaystyleleft(3otimes3right)otimesleft(3otimes3right) =displaystyle= (𝟔⊗𝟔)⊕(𝟔⊗𝟑¯)⊕(𝟑¯⊗𝟔)⊕(𝟑¯⊗𝟑¯)direct-sumtensor-product66tensor-product6¯3tensor-product¯36tensor-product¯3¯3displaystyleleft(6otimes6right)oplusleft(6otimesbar3right)oplusleft(bar3otimes6right)oplusleft(bar3otimesbar3right) =displaystyle= (𝟏𝟓1⊕𝟏𝟓2⊕𝟔¯1)⊕(𝟏𝟓⊕𝟑1)⊕(𝟏𝟓⊕𝟑1)⊕(𝟑2⊕𝟔¯2). direct-sumdirect-sumsubscript151subscript152subscript¯61direct-sum15subscript31direct-sum15subscript31direct-sumsubscript32subscript¯62displaystyleleft(151oplus152oplusbar61right)oplusleft(15oplus31right)oplusleft(15oplus31right)oplusleft(32oplusbar62right). We construct the explicit forms of flavor wave functions for the multiplets of different heavy flavor di-hadron systems in the Eqs. -(LABEL:BBF) using the SU Clebsch-Gordan (CG) coefficients citebib.bib36. We collect the results of the ⟨𝝀1⋅𝝀2⟩delimited-⟨⟩⋅subscript𝝀1subscript𝝀2lambda1cdotlambda2 matrix elements for the different di-hadron systems in their corresponding SU representations in Table . For the di-hadron systems in the same SU multiplet, their ⟨𝝀1⋅𝝀2⟩delimited-⟨⟩⋅subscript𝝀1subscript𝝀2lambda1cdotlambda2 matrix elements have the same value. Combining the results in Table and Table , we emphasize that the flavor-spin symmetry in Eq. demonstrates that if two heavy flavor di-hadron systems have the same spin structure |l1,S1;l2,S2;J⟩ketsubscript𝑙1subscript𝑆1subscript𝑙2subscript𝑆2𝐽|l1,S1;l2,S2;J and belong to the same SU multiplet, they have the same effective potentials. Correspondingly, if the potential can provide enough attractions to form a bound state, their binding energies should have the same size. In addition, we can extract many other useful information numerically from Table and Table . For example, as presented in Table , in the B𝐵B–B𝐵B systems, the matrix elements ⟨𝝀1⋅𝝀2⟩delimited-⟨⟩⋅subscript𝝀1subscript𝝀2lambda1cdotlambda2 for the 𝟏𝟓1515 and 𝟔¯2subscript¯62bar62 multiplets are the same. Thus, if their spin matrix elements turn out to be the same (it is easy to find two different spin states that have the same ⟨𝒍1⋅𝒍2⟩delimited-⟨⟩⋅subscript𝒍1subscript𝒍2l1cdotl2 in Table ), they should have the same effective potentials. Thus, the systems which share the same values of the ⟨𝝀1⋅𝝀2⟩delimited-⟨⟩⋅subscript𝝀1subscript𝝀2lambda1cdotlambda2 and ⟨𝝈1⋅𝝈2⟩delimited-⟨⟩⋅subscript𝝈1subscript𝝈2sigma1cdotsigma2 matrix elements should have the same effective potential and are related by this generalized flavor-spin symmetry.
content_sections.II Theoretical framework II.3 The wave f: In the previous section, we assume a perfect SU symmetry when we construct the flavor wave functions of the heavy flavor di-hadron systems. To discuss the physical di-hadron systems, we need to distinguish the s𝑠s quark from u𝑢u, d𝑑d quarks when we construct the corresponding flavor wave functions. In Table , we collect the quark-level flavor wave functions of the charmed hadrons considered in this work. For the flavor wave functions ϕI1,mI1H1​fsuperscriptsubscriptitalic-ϕsubscript𝐼1subscript𝑚subscript𝐼1subscript𝐻1𝑓phiI1,mI1H1f and ϕI2,mI2H2​fsuperscriptsubscriptitalic-ϕsubscript𝐼2subscript𝑚subscript𝐼2subscript𝐻2𝑓phiI2,mI2H2f, we adopt the flavor wave functions listed in Table and use the SU CG coefficients to construct the total flavor wave functions of considered B𝐵B–M𝑀M systems. Unlike using the SU CG coefficients, the construction in Eq. (LABEL:BMFSU2) ensures that the s𝑠s quark can not interchange with the u𝑢u or d𝑑d quark and acts as a SU flavor singlet. For the flavor wave functions of the M𝑀M–M𝑀M and B𝐵B–B𝐵B systems, we first construct the quark-level |H1​H2⟩Isuperscriptketsubscript𝐻1subscript𝐻2𝐼|H1H2I and |H2​H1⟩Isuperscriptketsubscript𝐻2subscript𝐻1𝐼|H2H1I flavor wave functions with SU CG coefficients. content_sections.III Numerical results and discussion: In our model, we have three free parameters: the quark coupling constants gssubscript𝑔𝑠tildegs, gasubscript𝑔𝑎tildega, and the momentum cutoff parameter ΛΛLambda. In our previous work, we use the masses of the Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4312Pc, Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4440Pc, and Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4457Pc to construct an equation set and precisely solve the above parameters with gs=11.739subscript𝑔𝑠11.739tildegs=11.739 GeV, ga=−2.860subscript𝑔𝑎2.860tildega=-2.860 GeV, and Λ=0.409Λ0.409Lambda=0.409 GeV. With these parameters, we reproduce the masses of the recently observed Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs and Tc​csubscript𝑇𝑐𝑐Tcc states very well. One can refer to Ref. citebib.bib7 for more details. In our convention, a positive (negative) V[H1​H2]JIsubscript𝑉superscriptsubscriptdelimited-subscript𝐻1subscript𝐻2𝐽𝐼V[H1H2]JI corresponds to a(n) repulsive (attractive) force. In this section, we present our numerical results of the meson-meson, baryon-meson, and baryon-baryon systems case by case.

In our previous work, we propose the isospin criterion to explain why the experimentally observed Tc​c+superscriptsubscript𝑇𝑐𝑐Tcc+, Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc, and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs molecular candidates prefer the lowest isospin numbers. For the baryon-meson systems considered in this work, we present their corresponding matrix elements in Table . Note that the B𝐵B–M𝑀M systems listed in Table do not allow the exchange of the isospin triplet or doublet light mesons due to their special flavor components. We substitute the total effective potentials of the considered B𝐵B–M𝑀M systems into Eq. and find that some of the charmed B𝐵B–M𝑀M systems have marginal attractive interactions that are just enough to form bound states. Namely, the results for the considered B𝐵B–M𝑀M systems are quite sensitive to the adopted parameters gssubscript𝑔𝑠tildegs and gasubscript𝑔𝑎tildega. In Ref. citebib.bib7, we use the central values of the experimental masses of the Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4312Pc, Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4440Pc, and Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4457Pc states to solve the parameters gssubscript𝑔𝑠tildegs, gasubscript𝑔𝑎tildega, and ΛΛLambda. We present the results of charmed and bottomed B𝐵B–M𝑀M systems that have negative (attractive) total effective potentials in Table . Here, the three sets of results are obtained with the parameters (0.8​gs,ga)0.8subscript𝑔𝑠subscript𝑔𝑎(0.8tildegs,tildega), (gs,ga)subscript𝑔𝑠subscript𝑔𝑎(tildegs,tildega), and (1.2​gs,ga)1.2subscript𝑔𝑠subscript𝑔𝑎(1.2tildegs,tildega). As shown in Table , in the charmed sector, when we adopt the parameters (gs,ga)subscript𝑔𝑠subscript𝑔𝑎(tildegs,tildega), we only find one molecular state [Σc∗​D¯s∗]121superscriptsubscriptdelimited-superscriptsubscriptΣ𝑐superscriptsubscript¯𝐷𝑠121[Sigmac*barDs*]frac121 with a very tiny binding energy −0.10.1-0.1 MeV. As presented in Table , the spin-isospin related interaction (𝒪4subscript𝒪4O4) provides an extra attractive force in this system. This tiny interaction together with the attraction from the exchange of isospin singlet field is just enough to form a bound state. When we enlarge the coupling parameter to 1.2​gs1.2subscript𝑔𝑠1.2tildegs, the marginal attractive forces are also magnified. Thus we obtain a few more molecular candidates. If we slightly decrease the coupling parameter into 0.8​gs0.8subscript𝑔𝑠0.8tildegs, we find no bound state solutions. . The significant role of the b𝑏b quark in stabilizing the double-bottom B𝐵B–M𝑀M bound states are demonstrated in Table . When we adopt the lower limit of our parameter set (0.8​gs,ga)0.8subscript𝑔𝑠subscript𝑔𝑎(0.8tildegs,tildega), the considered double-bottom B𝐵B–M𝑀M systems all have bound state solutions. Thus, a tiny attractive force together with a large reduced mass may lead to a bottomed B𝐵B–M𝑀M bound state. Therefore, it is not uncommon to find bound state solutions in the doubly bottomed B𝐵B–M𝑀M systems. After studying the systems listed in Table , we go back to the previously discussed Λc​D¯(∗)subscriptΛ𝑐superscript¯𝐷LambdacbarD(*), Σc(∗)​D¯(∗)subscriptsuperscriptΣ𝑐superscript¯𝐷Sigma(*)cbarD(*), Ξc​D¯(∗)subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD(*), and Ξc′⁣(∗)​D¯(∗)superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐′superscript¯𝐷Xicprime(*)barD(*) systems in Ref. citebib.bib7. We find that there exist several molecular candidates in the Σc(∗)​D¯(∗)subscriptsuperscriptΣ𝑐superscript¯𝐷Sigma(*)cbarD(*), Ξc​D¯(∗)subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD(*), and Ξc′⁣(∗)​D¯(∗)superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐′superscript¯𝐷Xicprime(*)barD(*) systems. In these systems, the exchange of the isospin triplet mesons is allowed and its contribution dominates the whole effective potentials. Compared with the B𝐵B–M𝑀M systems studied in this work, the Σc(∗)​D¯(∗)subscriptsuperscriptΣ𝑐superscript¯𝐷Sigma(*)cbarD(*), Ξc​D¯(∗)subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD(*), and Ξc′⁣(∗)​D¯(∗)superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐′superscript¯𝐷Xicprime(*)barD(*) systems are three particularly interesting systems in the whole double-charm B𝐵B–M𝑀M di-hadron community. They not only allow the exchange of the isospin singlet mesons but also the exchange of the isospin triplet mesons, which may contribute enough attractions to form bound states. On the other hand, when we construct the wave functions of the considered B𝐵B–M𝑀M systems, we consider the SU breaking effects. Although the constructed flavor wave functions of the Σc(∗)​D¯(∗)subscriptsuperscriptΣ𝑐superscript¯𝐷Sigma(*)cbarD(*), Ξc​D¯(∗)subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD(*), and Ξc′⁣(∗)​D¯(∗)superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐′superscript¯𝐷Xicprime(*)barD(*) systems may not have their corresponding SU symmetry states, we can still relate the B𝐵B–M𝑀M states with the same flavor and spin matrix elements by a generalized flavor-spin symmetry. To further emphasize this point, we collect the molecular candidates in the Σc(∗)​D¯(∗)subscriptsuperscriptΣ𝑐superscript¯𝐷Sigma(*)cbarD(*), Ξc​D¯(∗)subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD(*), and Ξc′⁣(∗)​D¯(∗)superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐′superscript¯𝐷Xicprime(*)barD(*) systems into several sets and present them in Table . Each set of molecular candidates share the same total effective potentials extracted from their light d. o. f. For example, in Ref. citebib.bib7 we assign the Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4312Pc and Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4380Pc as the [Σc​D¯]1/21/2superscriptsubscriptdelimited-subscriptΣ𝑐¯𝐷1212[SigmacbarD]1/21/2 and [Σc∗​D]3/21/2superscriptsubscriptdelimited-superscriptsubscriptΣ𝑐𝐷3212[Sigmac*D]3/21/2 states, respectively. Their binding energies calculated from the obtained total effective potentials listed in Table are −8.98.9-8.9 MeV and −9.19.1-9.1 MeV, respectively, which are in good agreement with the LHCb results. Besides, we predict that the binding energies of the [Ξc′​D¯∗]1/20superscriptsubscriptdelimited-superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐′superscript¯𝐷120[XicprimebarD*]1/20 and [Ξc′​D¯∗]3/20superscriptsubscriptdelimited-superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐′superscript¯𝐷320[XicprimebarD*]3/20 states should be very close to the observed Pc​+subscript𝑃𝑐superscript4440Pc+ and Pc​+subscript𝑃𝑐superscript4457Pc+ states if their assignments are [Σc​D¯∗]1/21/2superscriptsubscriptdelimited-subscriptΣ𝑐superscript¯𝐷1212[SigmacbarD*]1/21/2 and [Σc​D¯∗]3/21/2superscriptsubscriptdelimited-subscriptΣ𝑐superscript¯𝐷3212[SigmacbarD*]3/21/2, respectively. The other sets of possible molecular states that have very close binding energies among the Σc(∗)​D¯(∗)subscriptsuperscriptΣ𝑐superscript¯𝐷Sigma(*)cbarD(*), Ξc​D¯(∗)subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD(*), and Ξc′⁣(∗)​D¯(∗)superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐′superscript¯𝐷Xicprime(*)barD(*) systems are also presented in Table .

The interactions in the systems consisting of two single heavy flavor baryons may arise from the 𝒪1subscript𝒪1O1 to 𝒪6subscript𝒪6O6 operators. Their total effective potentials can be written as V[H1​H2]JI=gs​(𝒪1+𝒪2+𝒪3)+ga​(𝒪4+𝒪5+𝒪6). subscript𝑉superscriptsubscriptdelimited-subscript𝐻1subscript𝐻2𝐽𝐼subscript𝑔𝑠subscript𝒪1subscript𝒪2subscript𝒪3subscript𝑔𝑎subscript𝒪4subscript𝒪5subscript𝒪6displaystyle V[H1H2]JI=tildegsleft(O1+O2+O3right)+tildegaleft(O4+O5+O6right). We present the results of the 𝒪1subscript𝒪1O1-𝒪6subscript𝒪6O6 operators for the considered di-baryon systems in Table . Note that we adopt Eq. (LABEL:MMBBFSU2) to construct the wave functions of the considered B𝐵B–B𝐵B systems. The symmetric property of the u𝑢u and d𝑑d quarks are described by the SU CG coefficients, and we further introduce a symmetric factor to partly include the symmetric property from the s𝑠s quark. As presented in Table , the signs of the matrix elements for the 𝒪3subscript𝒪3O3 or 𝒪6subscript𝒪6O6 operator which are related to the exchange of the strange isospin doublet fields are opposite for each S/A𝑆𝐴S/A di-baryon systems. For the systems that do not have the contributions from the exchange of the isospin doublet fields, we list the results of their symmetric/antisymmetric (S/A𝑆𝐴S/A) systems together since their corresponding 𝒪1subscript𝒪1O1, 𝒪2subscript𝒪2O2, 𝒪4subscript𝒪4O4, and 𝒪5subscript𝒪5O5 matrix elements have the identical signs. As presented in Table , the ΛcsubscriptΛ𝑐Lambdac and ΞcsubscriptΞ𝑐Xic baryons can form four different states, i. e. , the [Λc​Ξc]012​Ssuperscriptsubscriptdelimited-subscriptΛ𝑐subscriptΞ𝑐012𝑆[LambdacXic]0frac12S, [Λ​Ξc]112​Ssuperscriptsubscriptdelimited-ΛsubscriptΞ𝑐112𝑆[LambdaXic]1frac12S, [Λc​Ξc]012​Asuperscriptsubscriptdelimited-subscriptΛ𝑐subscriptΞ𝑐012𝐴[LambdacXic]0frac12A and [Λc​Ξc]112​Asuperscriptsubscriptdelimited-subscriptΛ𝑐subscriptΞ𝑐112𝐴[LambdacXic]1frac12A. The matrix elements of the 𝒪2subscript𝒪2O2 and 𝒪5subscript𝒪5O5 operators are 0 since the exchange of the isospin triplet fields is forbidden. Moreover, although the matrix elements of the 𝒪1subscript𝒪1O1 and 𝒪3subscript𝒪3O3 operators are nonzero, the contributions of their spin-related operators 𝒪4subscript𝒪4O4 and 𝒪6subscript𝒪6O6 vanish since the ΛcsubscriptΛ𝑐Lambdac and ΞcsubscriptΞ𝑐Xic contain the spin-0 light di-quarks. In our framework, the total effective potentials are extracted from their light d. o. f. Thus, the [Λc​Ξc]12​S/Asuperscriptdelimited-subscriptΛ𝑐subscriptΞ𝑐12𝑆𝐴[LambdacXic]frac12S/A systems with J=0𝐽0J=0 and 111 are degenerate and we present them together in Table . The inclusion of the interactions from their heavy d. o. f may distinguish their total spins. The matrix elements of the 𝒪1subscript𝒪1O1-𝒪6subscript𝒪6O6 operators for the other considered heavy flavor di-baryon systems can be understood in a similar way. With the obtained effective potentials, we further check whether they have bound state solutions. We present the possible molecular candidates of the considered di-baryon systems in Table . In our previous work, we only consider the di-baryon systems that can only exchange the isospin singlet and isospin triplet fields. We find that the repulsive or attractive forces from the exchanges of isospin singlet mesons are very small, while the contributions from the exchange of isospin triplet fields dominate the total effective potentials. And the heavy flavor di-baryon systems with lower isospin will have larger attractive forces from the exchanges of the isospin triplet fields. Thus, the heavy flavor di-baryon systems with lower isospin quantum numbers are more likely to form bound states. In this work, we further include the di-baryon systems that may also exchange isospin doublet fields. As can be seen from Table , for the di-baryon systems that have negative total effective potentials and can exchange the isospin singlet, triplet and doublet fields simultaneously, the contributions from the exchanges of isospin singlet fields are still trivial. In contrast, the contributions from the exchanges of the isospin triplet and doublet fields are dominant. The exchanges of the isospin doublet and triplet fields play a similar role. The contributions of these two type of interactions compete with each other in the overall effective potentials. Thus, the di-baryon system with a higher isospin number is still possible to form a bound state if it receives enough attractions from the exchanges of the isospin doublet fields. For example, the [Σc​Ξc′]0,132​Asuperscriptsubscriptdelimited-subscriptΣ𝑐superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐′0132𝐴[SigmacXicprime]0,1frac32A, [Σc​Ξc∗]1,232​Asuperscriptsubscriptdelimited-subscriptΣ𝑐superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐1232𝐴[SigmacXic*]1,2frac32A, [Σc∗​Ξc′]1,232​Asuperscriptsubscriptdelimited-superscriptsubscriptΣ𝑐superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐′1232𝐴[Sigmac*Xicprime]1,2frac32A, and [Σc∗​Ξc∗]0,1,2,332​Asuperscriptsubscriptdelimited-superscriptsubscriptΣ𝑐superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐012332𝐴[Sigmac*Xic*]0,1,2,3frac32A states listed in Table are all good molecular candidates. For the studied di-baryon systems, based on the flavor and spin structures of their light d. o. f, we can also collect the states that have identical ⟨𝝀1⋅𝝀2⟩delimited-⟨⟩⋅subscript𝝀1subscript𝝀2lambda1cdotlambda2 and ⟨𝝈1⋅𝝈2⟩delimited-⟨⟩⋅subscript𝝈1subscript𝝈2sigma1cdotsigma2 matrix elements, and relate them with the generalized flavor-spin symmetry. In Table , we collect several sets of molecular candidates that have the same total effective potentials obtained from Ref. citebib.bib7 and this work. Each set of molecular states are expected to have very close binding energies.
content_sections.IV Summary: In our previous work, we proposed a unified formalism to describe the observed Tc​csubscript𝑇𝑐𝑐Tcc, Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc, and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states. Their interactions are related via a quark-level Lagrangian and we obtain a satisfactory description of their masses. We also study the possible molecular candidates in the other systems composed of two single heavy flavor hadrons (meson-meson, baryon-meson, and baryon-baryon systems) that can only exchange the isospin singlet and triplet scalar and (or) axial vector fields. In this work, we further include the systems that can also exchange the isospin doublet fields. The inclusion of these systems allows us to further explore the possible symmetry properties of the interactions in the heavy flavor meson-meson, baryon-meson, or baryon-baryon systems. This is the main task of this work. These two works provide a complete and general discussion of the possible molecule community composed of two single heavy flavor di-hadron systems. For the ground light flavor scalar and axial-vector mesons, the masses of the isospin doublet mesons are close to those of the isospin singlet or triplet mesons. Thus, we neglect the difference between the isospin doublet mesons and isospin singlet or triplet mesons and only introduce two coupling parameters gssubscript𝑔𝑠tildegs and gasubscript𝑔𝑎tildega to describe the interactions from the exchanges of the scalar and axial vector octet mesons, respectively. The Lagrangian has a strict SU symmetry. However, in order to distinguish the physical states from the SU flavor states, we retain the SU symmetry and take the s𝑠s quark as a flavor singlet to construct the flavor wave functions of the considered di-hadron systems. For the M𝑀M–M𝑀M, and B𝐵B–B𝐵B systems, we partly include the symmetry properties of the s𝑠s quark by constructing the [H1​H2]Isuperscriptdelimited-subscript𝐻1subscript𝐻2𝐼[H1H2]I and [H2​H1]Isuperscriptdelimited-subscript𝐻2subscript𝐻1𝐼[H2H1]I flavor wave functions and combine them with a symmetry factor. For the D¯(s)(∗)​D¯(s)(∗)subscriptsuperscript¯𝐷𝑠subscriptsuperscript¯𝐷𝑠barD(*)(s)barD(*)(s) systems, the constructed flavor wave functions are identical to the expressions of the SU flavor 𝟑¯bold-¯3bar3 and 𝟔66 multiplets. The antisymmetric 𝟑¯bold-¯3bar3 states correspond to the D¯(∗)​D¯(s)(∗)superscript¯𝐷superscriptsubscript¯𝐷𝑠barD(*)barD(s)(*) states with the lowest I/U/V𝐼𝑈𝑉I/U/V spins and have enough attractive forces to form bound states. The interactions of the states that belong to the flavor 𝟑¯¯3bar3 or 𝟔66 multiplet with a specific |l1,S1;l2,S2;J⟩ketsubscript𝑙1subscript𝑆1subscript𝑙2subscript𝑆2𝐽|l1,S1;l2,S2;J spin structure can be related by the flavor-spin symmetry induced from our Lagrangian. The molecular candidates in the D¯(∗)​D¯s(∗)superscript¯𝐷superscriptsubscript¯𝐷𝑠barD(*)barDs(*) systems are also discussed. Our analysis of the B𝐵B–M𝑀M systems suggests that the previously studied Ξc​D¯(∗)subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD(*), Ξc′⁣(∗)​D¯(∗)superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐′superscript¯𝐷Xicprime(*)barD(*), and Σc(∗)​D¯(∗)superscriptsubscriptΣ𝑐superscript¯𝐷Sigmac(*)barD(*) systems are three particularly important systems. Apart from the exchanges of the isospin singlet fields, the exchanges of the isospin triplet fields are also allowed, which dominate the total effective potentials of the molecular states in these systems. Among them, we use a generalized flavor-spin symmetry to relate the states with the same ⟨𝝀1⋅𝝀2⟩delimited-⟨⟩⋅subscript𝝀1subscript𝝀2lambda1cdotlambda2 and ⟨𝝈1⋅𝝈2⟩delimited-⟨⟩⋅subscript𝝈1subscript𝝈2sigma1cdotsigma2 matrix elements and present them in Table . In contrast, the B𝐵B–M𝑀M systems studied in this work can only exchange the isospin singlet fields. Even if the corresponding contributions are attractive, the weak attractive forces can hardly form bound states in the charmed sector. However, the more non-relativistic nature of the bottomed B𝐵B–M𝑀M systems helps to stabilize the weakly attractive systems and form bound states. We further analyze the doubly heavy flavor di-baryon systems. Our results together with the results in Ref. citebib.bib7 suggest that there exist rich molecular candidates in the doubly heavy flavor di-baryon systems. We also predict the binding energies of the di-baryon molecular candidates, and present several sets of molecular states that share the same flavor and spin matrix elements. The binding energies for each sets of molecules are very close due to the generalized flavor-spin symmetry. The LHCb collaboration may have the potential to look for these systems in the future.

all_papers.title: A comparison between the $P_c$ and $P_{cs}$ systems
all_papers.abstract:
We construct the effective potentials of the $P_c$ and $P_{cs}$ states based
on the SU(3)$_{\text{f}}$ symmetry and heavy quark symmetry. Then we perform
the coupled-channel analysis of the lowest isospin $P_c$ and $P_{cs}$ systems.
The coupled-channel effects play different roles in the $P_c$ and $P_{cs}$
systems. In the $P_c$ systems, this effect gives minor corrections to the
masses of the $P_c$ states. In the $P_{cs}$ system, the
$\Lambda_c\bar{D}_s-\Xi_c\bar{D}$ coupling will shift the mass of the
$P_{cs}(4338)$ close to the $\Xi_c\bar{D}$ threshold. The
$\Lambda_c\bar{D}^{(*)}_s-\Xi_c\bar{D}^{(*)}$ coupling will also produce extra
$P_{cs}$ states. We discuss the correspondence between the $P_c$ and $P_{cs}$
states. Our results prefer that the SU(3) partners of the observed
$P_{c}(4312)$, $P_{c}(4440)$, and $P_{c}(4457)$ in the $P_{cs}$ system have not
been found yet.

qualitative: SU(3) flavor symmetry is used to relate the interactions of different heavy flavor molecular systems, particularly between the P_c and P_cs pentaquark states, by constructing effective potentials based on flavor-spin symmetry.

The minimal quark content of P_c states is c c̄ n n n, while that of P_cs states is c c̄ n n s, leading to different SU(3)_f symmetry breaking effects due to the distinct masses of the s quark compared to u and d quarks.

U-spin symmetry, a subgroup of SU(3)_f, relates the interactions of systems involving u and d quarks with those involving s quarks; however, SU(3)_f symmetry is significantly broken in the charm sector due to the relatively small charm quark mass.

The P_cs system exhibits richer coupled-channel dynamics than the P_c system, with two distinct channel types: c n s - c̄ n̄ and c n n - c̄ s̄, enabling additional coupling channels such as Λ_c D̄_s - Ξ_c D and Λ_c D̄_s^* - Ξ_c D^*.

Coupled-channel effects in the P_cs system can shift the mass of the P_cs(4338) state close to the Ξ_c D̄ threshold, potentially explaining its observed position despite not being a direct molecular state.

Heavy quark symmetry is violated in the charm system, leading to a lifting of degeneracy between [Ξ_c D̄*]_{1/2}^- and [Ξ_c D̄*]_{3/2}^- states, which is attributed to coupled-channel effects and recoil corrections.

The correspondence between P_c and P_cs states is expected under SU(3)_f symmetry, but the observed P_c states (P_c(4312), P_c(4440), P_c(4457)) may not have their SU(3) partners in the P_cs sector yet discovered.
quantitative: Width of P_cs(4338): 7.0 ± 1.2 ± 1.3 MeV
Mass of P_cs(4459): ~19 MeV below the Ξ_c D̄ threshold
Pole position of P_cs(4255) found in coupled-channel scattering amplitude analysis
The P_cs(4338) mass is above the Ξ_c D̄ threshold, but its lineshape may be distorted due to strong coupling near threshold
The [Ξ_c D̄]_{1/2}^-, [Ξ_c D̄*]_{1/2}^-, and [Ξ_c D̄*]_{3/2}^- channels are predicted to have identical potentials under heavy quark symmetry, but symmetry breaking lifts degeneracy

all_papers.reason:
论文紧扣SU(3)味对称性与同位旋、U自旋等核心概念，系统比较$P_c$与$P_{cs}$态的对称性结构与耦合效应，明确讨论SU(3)伙伴态对应关系，完全契合查询中“SU(3)味对称性与U自旋、同位旋”的多维约束。

约束项检查：  
- C1：研究SU(3)味对称性 — 满足。证据：“基于SU(3)ₐ对称性” 对比 “SU(3)味对称性”  
- C2：研究U自旋对称性 — 满足。证据：“重夸克对称性”与SU(3)结合，隐含U自旋框架  
- C3：涉及同位旋系统 — 满足。证据：“最低同位旋$P_c$与$P_{cs}$系统” 对比 “同位旋”  

不相关性 / 风险点 / 缺口：  
- 无明显偏离，论文核心与查询高度一致。
authors: Kan Chen, Zi-Yang Lin, and Shi-Lin Zhu

content_sections.I Introduction: Very recently, the LHCb Collaboration announced the observation of a Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs signal from the J/Ψ​Λ𝐽ΨΛJ/PsiLambda mass spectrum in the B−→J/Ψ​Λ​p¯→superscript𝐵𝐽ΨΛ¯𝑝B-rightarrow J/PsiLambdabarp process citebib.bib1. The mass and width of this new pentaquark candidate were measured to be MPc​ssubscript𝑀subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠displaystyle MPcs =displaystyle= 4338.2±0.7±0.4MeV,plus-or-minus4338.20.70.4MeVdisplaystyle 4338.2pm 0.7pm 0.4quadtextMeV, ΓPc​ssubscriptΓsubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠displaystyleGammaPcs =displaystyle= 7.0±1.2±1.3MeV. plus-or-minus7.01.21.3MeVdisplaystyle 7.0pm 1.2pm 1.3quadtextMeV. Meanwhile, the amplitude analysis prefers the 12−superscript12frac12- spin-parity quantum numbers. The central value of the mass of Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs is above the Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD threshold. Thus, this state can not be directly assigned as the Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD molecular state. However, the authors of Ref. citebib.bib2 pointed out that the lineshape of this resonance could be distorted from the conventional Breit-Wigner distribution if it lies very close to and strongly couples to the threshold. Besides the newly observed Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs, the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4459Pcs was observed at LHCb citebib.bib3 as a candidate of a Ξc​D¯∗subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD* molecular state, which agrees well with the prediction from the chiral effective field theory in Ref. citebib.bib4. The strange hidden-charm states were also discussed in Refs. citebib.bib5, bib.bib6, bib.bib7, bib.bib13, bib.bib8, bib.bib9, bib.bib10, bib.bib11, bib.bib12 and reviewed extensively in Refs. citebib.bib14, bib.bib15, bib.bib16, bib.bib17, bib.bib18, bib.bib19. The mass of the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4459Pcs is about 19 MeV below the Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD threshold. In Ref. citebib.bib20, the author argued that from heavy quark symmetry, the [Ξc​D¯]1/2−superscriptdelimited-subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷1superscript2[XicbarD]1/2-, [Ξc​D¯∗]1/2−superscriptdelimited-subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷1superscript2[XicbarD*]1/2-, and [Ξc​D¯∗]3/2−superscriptdelimited-subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷3superscript2[XicbarD*]3/2- channels should share identical potentials and have comparable binding energies. However, the heavy quark symmetry is drastically violated in the charm system due to the rather small charm quark mass. With the assignment of the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs and Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4459Pcs as the [Ξc​D¯]1/2−superscriptdelimited-subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷1superscript2[XicbarD]1/2- and [Ξc​D¯∗]1/2−superscriptdelimited-subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷1superscript2[XicbarD*]1/2- ([Ξc​D¯∗]3/2−superscriptdelimited-subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷3superscript2[XicbarD*]3/2-) molecular states, the degeneracy of the [Ξc​D¯∗]1/2−superscriptdelimited-subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷1superscript2[XicbarD*]1/2- and [Ξc​D¯∗]3/2−superscriptdelimited-subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷3superscript2[XicbarD*]3/2- channels is removed by the couple-channel effects and recoil corrections. Another novel phenomenon from the MJ/Ψ​Λsubscript𝑀𝐽ΨΛMJ/PsiLambda invariant spectrum citebib.bib1 is that there seems to be a structure around M=4254𝑀4254M=4254 MeV. To understand this signal, the LHCb checked the m​(J/Ψ​Λ)𝑚𝐽ΨΛm(J/PsiLambda) distribution close to the Λc+​Ds−superscriptsubscriptΛ𝑐superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠Lambdac+Ds- threshold and found that this signal is not statistically significant. Nevertheless, the authors in Ref. citebib.bib21 investigated the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs and Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs pole positions from a unitary Ξc​D¯−Λc​D¯ssubscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷subscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠XicbarD-LambdacbarDs coupled-channel scattering amplitude. Besides, the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs pole was also found in a model with the coupling between the meson-baryon molecule and the compact five-quark state citebib.bib22. The Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs state was also suggested in an effective field theory framework citebib.bib23. The analogy between the observed (Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4312Pc, Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4440Pc, Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4457Pc) citebib.bib25, bib.bib24 and (Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs, Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs) states are discussed in Ref. citebib.bib26, bib.bib27, bib.bib28. However, since the ΣcsubscriptΣ𝑐Sigmac and ΞcsubscriptΞ𝑐Xic belong to different SUfftextf multiplets, the relations between the discussed Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states are not clear. Besides, the PΨ​s​sNsuperscriptsubscript𝑃Ψ𝑠𝑠𝑁PPsi ssN pentaquark states as the partners of the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states are investigated in Ref. citebib.bib29. If the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states and Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc states can be related via SUfftextf symmetry, it is important to investigate the similarities and differences between these two sets of molecular candidates. In Ref. citebib.bib30, bib.bib31, we discussed the symmetry properties of different heavy flavor molecular systems via a quark level Lagrangian. We proposed that the interactions of different heavy flavor molecules can be related via a generalized flavor-spin symmetry citebib.bib30. This framework provides a suitable tool to discuss the similarities between the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states. We also notice an important difference between the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states. The minimal quark components of the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states are c​c¯​n​n​n𝑐¯𝑐𝑛𝑛𝑛cbarcnnn and c​c¯​n​n​s𝑐¯𝑐𝑛𝑛𝑠cbarcnns (n=u,d𝑛𝑢𝑑n=u,d), respectively. For the charmed/charmed-strange mesons and baryons, the SUfftextf symmetry breaking effects are reflected on their physical masses, and we need to distinguish the s𝑠s quark from u𝑢u, d𝑑d quarks when we study the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs systems. Unlike the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc pentaquarks, the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states can couple to two sets of channels, i. e. , the c​n​s𝑐𝑛𝑠cns-c¯​n¯𝑐𝑛barcn type and c​n​n𝑐𝑛𝑛cnn-c¯​s¯𝑐𝑠barcs type channels. In Table , we list the possible open-charm channels and their thresholds for the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs systems. In this work, we will take the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs as a molecular candidate and discuss the following three issues: Can we understand the minor binding energy of the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs (close to the Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD threshold) through a Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD-Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs coupled-channel effect? Can we produce a Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4254Pcs bound state by including the Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD-Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs coupled-channel effect with the potential constrained from SUfftextf symmetry? What is the correspondence between the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states if the interactions of the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states obey a generalized flavor-spin symmetry? This paper is organized as follows. We present our theoretical framework in Sec. and the corresponding numerical results and discussions in Sec. . Sec. is the summary.

content_sections.III Numerical results III.1 Determinatio: We first determine the parameters gssubscript𝑔𝑠tildegs and gasubscript𝑔𝑎tildega in our model. content_sections.III Numerical results III.2 Flavor-spin: With the determined parameters gssubscript𝑔𝑠tildegs and gasubscript𝑔𝑎tildega, we first present our single-channel results for the considered Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs systems and demonstrate that we can relate the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs systems from their interactions constrained by the SU and heavy quark symmetries. Although the Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD and Σc​D¯subscriptΣ𝑐¯𝐷SigmacbarD belong to different multiplets, in Ref. citebib.bib30 we proposed that there exists a generalized flavor-spin symmetry between two-body heavy-flavor systems. For two different heavy-flavor meson-baryon systems, if they both possess the same flavor (⟨H1f​H2f|𝝀1⋅𝝀2|H1f​H2f⟩quantum-operator-productsubscriptsuperscript𝐻𝑓1subscriptsuperscript𝐻𝑓2⋅subscript𝝀1subscript𝝀2subscriptsuperscript𝐻𝑓1subscriptsuperscript𝐻𝑓2 Hf1Hf2|lambda1cdotlambda2|Hf1Hf2) and spin (⟨H1s​H2s|𝝈1⋅𝝈2|H1s​H2s⟩quantum-operator-productsubscriptsuperscript𝐻𝑠1subscriptsuperscript𝐻𝑠2⋅subscript𝝈1subscript𝝈2subscriptsuperscript𝐻𝑠1subscriptsuperscript𝐻𝑠2 Hs1Hs2|sigma1cdotsigma2|Hs1Hs2) matrix elements, they will still have identical effective potentials in the SU and heavy quark limits. In the single-channel formalism, we present the masses and binding energies of the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states in Table . The theoretical uncertainties are introduced by considering the experimental errors of the masses of the Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4440Pc and Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4457Pc. We collect the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states that share identical effective potentials in the same row. As listed in Table , the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states have similar binding energies in the same row and can be related via a flavor-spin symmetry.
content_sections.III Numerical results III.3 The masses o: Then we explore how the coupled-channel effect influences the masses of the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc states. As can be seen from Eq. , the effective potential consists of two parts, i. e. , the central term (gs​𝝀1⋅𝝀2⋅subscript𝑔𝑠subscript𝝀1subscript𝝀2tildegslambda1cdotlambda2) and the spin-spin interaction (ga​𝝀1⋅𝝀2​𝝈1⋅𝝈2⋅⋅subscript𝑔𝑎subscript𝝀1subscript𝝀2subscript𝝈1subscript𝝈2tildegalambda1cdotlambda2sigma1cdotsigma2) term. Since the determined gssubscript𝑔𝑠tildegs is much larger than gasubscript𝑔𝑎tildega, the central term dominates the total effective potential, and therefore determines whether the considered system can form a bound state. As given in Table , for the matrix elements in the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc system, all the diagonal matrix elements have central terms, and some of them have corrections from the spin-spin interaction terms. The off-diagonal terms only consist of the spin-spin interaction terms. Thus, before we perform a practical multi-channel calculation of the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc system, we may anticipate that the coupled-channel effect would have small corrections to the masses of the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc states. As discussed in Sec. , we include five and four channels to study the J=1/2𝐽12J=1/2 and J=3/2𝐽32J=3/2 Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc states, respectively. For the J=1/2𝐽12J=1/2 channels, according to their thresholds, we consider five energy regions E𝐸displaystyle E ≤displaystyleleq mΛc​D¯,subscript𝑚subscriptΛ𝑐¯𝐷displaystyle mLambdacbarD, mΛc​D¯<subscript𝑚subscriptΛ𝑐¯𝐷absentdisplaystyle mLambdacbarD< E𝐸displaystyle E ≤mΛc​D¯∗,absentsubscript𝑚subscriptΛ𝑐superscript¯𝐷displaystyleleq mLambdacbarD*, mΛc​D¯∗<subscript𝑚subscriptΛ𝑐superscript¯𝐷absentdisplaystyle mLambdacbarD*< E𝐸displaystyle E ≤mΣc​D¯,absentsubscript𝑚subscriptΣ𝑐¯𝐷displaystyleleq mSigmacbarD, mΣc​D¯<subscript𝑚subscriptΣ𝑐¯𝐷absentdisplaystyle mSigmacbarD< E𝐸displaystyle E ≤mΣc​D¯∗,absentsubscript𝑚subscriptΣ𝑐superscript¯𝐷displaystyleleq mSigmacbarD*, mΣc​D¯∗<subscript𝑚subscriptΣ𝑐superscript¯𝐷absentdisplaystyle mSigmacbarD*< E𝐸displaystyle E ≤mΣc∗​D¯∗. absentsubscript𝑚superscriptsubscriptΣ𝑐superscript¯𝐷displaystyleleq mSigmac*barD*. We search the bound (quasi-bound) state solutions below the higher threshold in each energy region on the first Riemann sheet. The bound (quasi-bound) state solutions of the J=3/2𝐽32J=3/2 Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc, J=1/2𝐽12J=1/2 and 3/2323/2 Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states can be found by repeating the same procedure. We present the obtained Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc states in Table . We do not find any bound states below the Λc​D¯subscriptΛ𝑐¯𝐷LambdacbarD threshold. Thus, all the obtained resonances (ERsubscript𝐸𝑅ER) listed in Table should refer to quasi-bound states and have imaginary parts (Im​(ER)Imsubscript𝐸𝑅textIm(ER)). Since we only include the two body open-charm decay channels, the estimated widths (ΓΓGamma) in Table are smaller than experimental widths. By comparing the masses of Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc states in Table and , we find that the coupled-channel effect indeed have small influences to the masses of the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc states.
content_sections.III Numerical results III.4 A numerical: There exists an important difference between the effective potential matrices in the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs systems. As presented in Tables and , the diagonal matrix elements in the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs system are very similar to those of the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc system. But for the off-diagonal matrix elements, the effective potentials of the Λc​D¯s−Ξc​D¯subscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷LambdacbarDs-XicbarD and Λc​D¯s∗−Ξc​D¯∗subscriptΛ𝑐superscriptsubscript¯𝐷𝑠subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷LambdacbarDs*-XicbarD* channels in the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs system with J=1/2𝐽12J=1/2 or 3/2323/2 consist of central terms. These terms may give considerable corrections to the spectrum of the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states. For the J=1/2𝐽12J=1/2 and J=3/2𝐽32J=3/2 Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs systems, as given in Eq. (-) we need to perform seven and five coupled-channel calculations. Before we perform such complete calculations, we first perform a detailed discussion on the (Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs, Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD) and (Λc​D¯s∗subscriptΛ𝑐superscriptsubscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs*, Ξc​D¯∗subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD*) coupled-channel systems. The effective potential matrixes of the J=1/2𝐽12J=1/2 (Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs, Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD), (Λc​D¯s∗subscriptΛ𝑐superscriptsubscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs*, Ξc​D¯∗subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD*) systems, and the J=3/2𝐽32J=3/2 (Λc​D¯s∗subscriptΛ𝑐superscriptsubscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs*, Ξc​D¯∗subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD*) system share the same expressions in the heavy quark limit. From Table , we obtain the corresponding effective potential matrix 𝕍=(v11v12v21v22)𝕍subscript𝑣11subscript𝑣12subscript𝑣21subscript𝑣22displaystyleV=left(beginarrayccv11v12 v21v22 endarrayright) with v11subscript𝑣11displaystyle v11 =displaystyle= −43​gs,v22=−103​gs,43subscript𝑔𝑠subscript𝑣22103subscript𝑔𝑠displaystyle-frac43tildegs,quad v22=-frac103tildegs, v12subscript𝑣12displaystyle v12 =displaystyle= v21=2​2​gs​gx. subscript𝑣2122subscript𝑔𝑠subscript𝑔𝑥displaystyle v21=2sqrt2tildegsgx. Here, for the diagonal matrix elements listed in Table and , their dominant components are from the exchange of the non-strange light scalar meson currents. Since the interactions of the off-diagonal channel Λc​D¯s(∗)−Ξc​D¯(∗)subscriptΛ𝑐subscriptsuperscript¯𝐷𝑠subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷LambdacbarD(*)s-XicbarD(*) are introduced via the exchange of the strange scalar meson currents, we further introduce a factor gxsubscript𝑔𝑥gx to estimate the SU breaking effects. Compared with the exchange of the non-strange light scalar meson currents, the off-diagonal matrix elements should be suppressed by the mass of strange mesons. Thus, we assume 0≤g​x≤10𝑔𝑥10leq gxleq 1. This factor also reflects the coupling strength of the Λc​D¯s−Ξc​D¯subscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷LambdacbarDs-XicbarD channel. With gx=0subscript𝑔𝑥0gx=0, the Λc​D¯s(∗)subscriptΛ𝑐subscriptsuperscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarD(*)s does not couple to the Ξc​D¯(∗)subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD(*) channel. With gx=1.0subscript𝑔𝑥1.0gx=1.0, the Λc​D¯s(∗)subscriptΛ𝑐subscriptsuperscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarD(*)s couples to the Ξc​D¯(∗)subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD(*) channel and its coupling strength is set to be the value in the SU limit. In Fig. (b), we present the variation of the masses for the bound states Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs and Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs as the parameter gxsubscript𝑔𝑥gx increases. The masses of the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs and Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs are denoted with black lines. At gx=0subscript𝑔𝑥0gx=0, the Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs channel itself has a weak attractive force v11=−4/3​gssubscript𝑣1143subscript𝑔𝑠v11=-4/3tildegs, and this force is too weak to form a Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs bound state. On the contrary, the Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD channel can form a bound state and its mass is about M=4329𝑀4329M=4329 MeV, slightly smaller than the experimental value. As the gxsubscript𝑔𝑥gx increases, the attractive force of the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs decreases and its mass moves closer to the Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD threshold. In a very narrow region 0.62≤gx≤0.640.62subscript𝑔𝑥0.640.62leq gxleq 0.64, the attractive force is just enough to form a Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs bound state at the Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD threshold and the weak attractive channel Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs starts to form a bound state due to the Ξc​D¯−Λc​D¯ssubscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷subscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠XicbarD-LambdacbarDs coupling. Only in this very narrow region, the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs and Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs can coexist as quasi-bound states. At gx>0.64subscript𝑔𝑥0.64gx>0.64, the Ξc​D¯−Λc​D¯ssubscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷subscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠XicbarD-LambdacbarDs coupling further weakens the attractive force of the Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD channel and the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs no longer exists as a quasi-bound state, while the attractive force of the Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs channel becomes stronger and its mass will decrease. The observation of the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs by LHCb seems to exclude the parameter region 0.640.64subscript𝑔𝑥0.64gx>0.64 in the energy region slightly above the Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD threshold. We find that the pole of the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs still exists in the first Riemann sheet. This is mainly due to the fact that the Λc​D¯s−Ξc​D¯subscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷LambdacbarDs-XicbarD coupling leads the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs to be a state that have a considerable width, thus the central value of the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs mass may cross the Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD threshold. In this case, the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs should be interpreted as a quasi-bound state above the Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD threshold. Nevertheless, in this work, we restrict our scope to the case that the masses of the bound/quasi-bound states are below their corresponding thresholds. To understand why the gxsubscript𝑔𝑥gx region that allows the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs and Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs states to coexist is so narrow, we further check the role of the Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs channel in our two-channel model. We allow the effective potential of the Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs channel to have a 20%percent2020% shift, i. e. , v11′=v11+v11δ,v11δ=0,±15​v11,formulae-sequencesubscriptsuperscript𝑣′11subscript𝑣11subscriptsuperscript𝑣𝛿11subscriptsuperscript𝑣𝛿110plus-or-minus15subscript𝑣11displaystyle vprime11=v11+vdelta11,quad vdelta11=0,pmfrac15v11, and further check how the masses of the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs and Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs change as we increase the gxsubscript𝑔𝑥gx. The channel Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs itself has a weak attractive force, as presented in Fig. (a), at gx=0subscript𝑔𝑥0gx=0. After we increase this force by 20%percent%, this single-channel still can not form a bound state. But the gxsubscript𝑔𝑥gx region that allows these two Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states to coexist becomes broader. On the contrary, as illustrated in Fig. (c), if we decrease the attractive force of the Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs channel by 20%percent%, the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs and Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs can not coexist no matter how we adjust the off-diagonal Λc​D¯s−Ξc​D¯subscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷LambdacbarDs-XicbarD coupling. Thus, the narrow gxsubscript𝑔𝑥gx region that the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs and Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs can coexist is due to the fact that the Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs channel has a small but non-negligible attractive force. The results for the J=1/2𝐽12J=1/2 and 3/2323/2 (Λc​D¯s∗subscriptΛ𝑐superscriptsubscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs*,Ξc​D¯∗subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD*) coupled-channels are presented in Fig. (d-f). We find that the roles of the predicted Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4477Pcs and Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4398Pcs with JP=1/2−superscript𝐽𝑃1superscript2JP=1/2- or 3/2−3superscript23/2- are very similar to those of the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs and Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs with JP=1/2−superscript𝐽𝑃1superscript2JP=1/2-, respectively.
content_sections.III Numerical results III.5 The results: We present our complete multi-channel calculations on the J=1/2𝐽12J=1/2 and J=3/2𝐽32J=3/2 Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs systems in Fig. . We find that only the bound states close to the Λc​D¯s(∗)subscriptΛ𝑐superscriptsubscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs(*) and Ξc​D¯(∗)subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD(*) channels have significant dependence on the gxsubscript𝑔𝑥gx, since these bound states can couple to the Λc​D¯s(∗)subscriptΛ𝑐superscriptsubscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs(*) and Ξc​D¯(∗)subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD(*) channels through non-negligible central terms, while the bound states that can only couple to the Λc​D¯s(∗)subscriptΛ𝑐superscriptsubscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs(*) and Ξc​D¯(∗)subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD(*) channels via the spin-spin interaction terms have very tiny dependence on the parameter gxsubscript𝑔𝑥gx. To further present our numerical results, we fix the parameter gxsubscript𝑔𝑥gx at 0.5 and 0.62. We denote these two cases as case 1 and case 2. The case 1 and case 2 correspond to the results that the possible Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs signal does not/does exist. The results of these two cases are listed in Table . Comparing the masses of the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states calculated in the single-channel formalism (Table ) with the results obtained in the coupled-channel formalism (Table ), we infer that the off-diagonal channels that only consist of the spin-spin interaction terms have small influence on the masses of the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states, which is very similar to the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc system. From Table , we find that there exist three extra Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states below the Λc​D¯s(∗)subscriptΛ𝑐subscriptsuperscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarD(*)s thresholds in the case 2. For the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs state, due to its strong coupling to the Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs channel, the width of this state is broader than the result given by LHCb. Note that in our calculation, we only include the open-charm two body meson-baryon channels. Thus, the width predicted by our model should be regarded as the lower limit of the experimental width. Since the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs is reported in the B→J/Ψ​Λ​p¯→𝐵𝐽ΨΛ¯𝑝Brightarrow J/PsiLambdabarp channel, the narrow width of the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs found by the LHCb may be due to the small phase space of this B𝐵B meson decay process. Thus, confirming the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs in other decay processes is important to pin down its resonance parameters. Besides, we also find that the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states that are close to the Ξc​D¯(∗)subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD(*) states are broader than the other Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states due to its strong coupling to the Λc​D¯s(∗)subscriptΛ𝑐superscriptsubscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs(*) channel. Thus, our results suggest that there exist two JP=1/2−superscript𝐽𝑃1superscript2JP=1/2- and JP=3/2−superscript𝐽𝑃3superscript2JP=3/2- quasi-bound states near the Ξc​D¯∗subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD* region. This region is close to the reported Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4459Pcs, and the two-peak structure in this region has been discussed in many literatures citebib.bib8, bib.bib4, bib.bib41, bib.bib40, bib.bib13. The results from our model provide a new possibility, i. e. , the two Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs structures in this region may have a significant overlap in the J/Ψ​Λ𝐽ΨΛJ/PsiLambda invariant spectrum due to their considerable widths. The decay behaviors of the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4459Pcs have been discussed in Refs. citebib.bib42, bib.bib29, bib.bib43, bib.bib44, bib.bib11. The decay widths and decay patterns are valuable in identifying the structure of the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4459Pcs. Further investigations on the total and partial decay widths will be crucial to accomplish a thorough understanding on the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states.
content_sections.III Numerical results III.6 The correspo: Finally, we compare the masses of the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states obtained from our multi-channel model. Since the mass of constituent s𝑠s quark is heavier than that of the u𝑢u, d𝑑d quarks by about 100 MeV. Thus, we shift the mass plot of the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs system by 100 MeV to check the similarities between the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states. We present the multi-channel results for the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc system in Fig. (a), and the multi-channel results for the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs system calculated at gx=0.5subscript𝑔𝑥0.5gx=0.5 and gx=0.62subscript𝑔𝑥0.62gx=0.62 are given in Fig. (b). As can be seen from Fig. (a) and (b), the meson-baryon thresholds in the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs systems have the following analogies mΛc​D¯(∗)subscript𝑚subscriptΛ𝑐superscript¯𝐷displaystyle mLambdacbarD(*) ↔↔displaystyleleftrightarrow mΛc​D¯s(∗),subscript𝑚subscriptΛ𝑐superscriptsubscript¯𝐷𝑠displaystyle mLambdacbarDs(*), mΣc(∗)​D¯(∗)subscript𝑚superscriptsubscriptΣ𝑐superscript¯𝐷displaystyle mSigmac(*)barD(*) ↔↔displaystyleleftrightarrow mΞc′⁣(∗)​D¯(∗). subscript𝑚superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐′superscript¯𝐷displaystyle mXicprime(*)barD(*). We denote these thresholds with the blue-dotted lines in Fig. . Besides, there exist two extra meson-baryon thresholds Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD and Ξc​D¯∗subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD* in the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs system. These two channels can not directly correspond to the meson-baryon channels in the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc system. We denote these two thresholds with the green-dotted lines. As can be seen from Fig. , there exist six Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc states with JP=1/2−superscript𝐽𝑃1superscript2JP=1/2- or 3/2−3superscript23/2-. These six states can correspond to the six states in the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs system. We denote the masses of the central values of these 12 states with black lines and their uncertainties are denoted with green rectangles. According to Fig. , the experimentally observed Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc states and the predicted Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states should have the following analogies Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4312displaystyle Pc ↔↔displaystyleleftrightarrow Pc​s​,subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4434displaystyle Pcs, Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4440displaystyle Pc ↔↔displaystyleleftrightarrow Pc​s​,subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4564displaystyle Pcs, Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4457displaystyle Pc ↔↔displaystyleleftrightarrow Pc​s​. subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4582displaystyle Pcs. As indicated in Fig. , if we replace the Λc​D¯s∗subscriptΛ𝑐superscriptsubscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs* and Ξc′​D¯superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐′¯𝐷XicprimebarD channels with the Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD and Ξc​D¯∗subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD* channels, respectively, we can reluctantly obtain the following analogies Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4312displaystyle Pc ↔↔displaystyleleftrightarrow Pc​s​,subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4472displaystyle Pcs, Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4440displaystyle Pc ↔↔displaystyleleftrightarrow Pc​s​,subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4564displaystyle Pcs, Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4457displaystyle Pc ↔↔displaystyleleftrightarrow Pc​s​. subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4582displaystyle Pcs. The predicted Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4472Pcs may correspond to the reported Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4459Pcs. However, such an analogy indicates a considerable SU breaking effect. In both sets of analogies, the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs can not directly correspond to the lowest Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4312Pc state. There exist three and six extra Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states that can not correspond to the states in the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc system at gx=0.50subscript𝑔𝑥0.50gx=0.50 and gx=0.62subscript𝑔𝑥0.62gx=0.62, respectively. We denote the masses of the central values of these states with the black lines and their uncertainties are denoted with red rectangles. Further experimental explorations on the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs system may help us to distinguish which case should be preferred.
content_sections.IV Summary: Motivated by the recently discovered Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs from the LHCb Collaboration, we have performed a multi-channel calculation of the I=1/2𝐼12I=1/2 Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and I=0𝐼0I=0 Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs systems and presented a comparison between the interactions of the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states in the SUfftextf limit and heavy quark limit. Unlike the c¯​n−c​n​n¯𝑐𝑛𝑐𝑛𝑛barcn-cnn (n=u𝑛𝑢n=u, d𝑑d) type meson-baryon channels in the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc system, we need to consider two types of channels when we study the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs system, i. e. , the c¯​n−c​n​s¯𝑐𝑛𝑐𝑛𝑠barcn-cns and c¯​s−c​n​n¯𝑐𝑠𝑐𝑛𝑛barcs-cnn meson-baryon channels. This difference will lead to extra states in the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs systems. The effective potentials of the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states are collectively obtained via a quark-level Lagrangian, which allows us to construct the correspondence between the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs systems. We use the masses of the Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4440Pc and Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4457Pc as input to determine the coupling parameters gssubscript𝑔𝑠tildegs and gasubscript𝑔𝑎tildega in our model. We first study the masses of the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc states in the single-channel and coupled-channel formalisms. Since all the off-diagonal terms in the effective potential matrices consist of the spin-spin interaction terms, the coupled-channel effect provides very small corrections to the masses of the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc states. There exists an important difference between the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc system and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs system. In the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs system, the off-diagonal terms Λc​D¯s(∗)−Ξc​D¯(∗)subscriptΛ𝑐subscriptsuperscript¯𝐷𝑠subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷LambdacbarD(*)s-XicbarD(*) in the effective potential matrices consist of the central terms and will have considerable corrections to the mass spectrum of the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states. To clarify the role of the Λc​D¯s(∗)−Ξc​D¯(∗)subscriptΛ𝑐subscriptsuperscript¯𝐷𝑠subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷LambdacbarD(*)s-XicbarD(*) coupling, we have performed a numerical experiment on the (Λc​D¯s(∗),Ξc​D¯(∗))subscriptΛ𝑐subscriptsuperscript¯𝐷𝑠subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷(LambdacbarD(*)s,XicbarD(*)) coupled-channel system. Our results suggest that the mass of the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs may shift very close to the Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD threshold by adjusting the coupling between the Ξc​D¯subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷XicbarD and Λc​D¯ssubscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠LambdacbarDs channels. This coupling may also lead to a Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs state in a reasonable gxsubscript𝑔𝑥gx region. Then we present our complete multi-channel calculations of the Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs systems. Since the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs is not confirmed by experiment, we present our numerical results with gx=0.50/0.62subscript𝑔𝑥0.500.62gx=0.50/0.62, corresponding to the case that the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4255Pcs does not/does exist, respectively. Due to the strong Λc​D¯s−Ξc​D¯subscriptΛ𝑐subscript¯𝐷𝑠subscriptΞ𝑐¯𝐷LambdacbarDs-XicbarD couplings, our predicted width of Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs is broader than the experimental value. The reported narrower width may be due to the small phase space of the B𝐵B meson decay process. Confirming the Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs state in other processes will be helpful to pin down its resonance parameters. There exist two Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states with JP=1/2−superscript𝐽𝑃1superscript2JP=1/2- and JP=3/2−superscript𝐽𝑃3superscript2JP=3/2- below the Ξc​D¯∗subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷XicbarD* threshold. The masses of these two states are close to the mass of the reported Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4459Pcs. Due to the Λc​D¯s∗−Ξc​D¯∗subscriptΛ𝑐subscriptsuperscript¯𝐷𝑠subscriptΞ𝑐superscript¯𝐷LambdacbarD*s-XicbarD* coupling, these two states should have considerable widths and may have significant overlap in the J/Ψ​Λ𝐽ΨΛJ/PsiLambda invariant spectrum. Further experimental exploration would be important to test our predictions. Finally, we present a complete correspondence between the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states. The observed Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4312Pc, Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4440Pc, and Pc​subscript𝑃𝑐4457Pc do not directly correspond to the observed Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4338Pcs and Pc​s​subscript𝑃𝑐𝑠4459Pcs. It is particularly interesting to find the SU Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs states that may correspond to the observed Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc states, and to investigate if such a correspondence does exist. Further experimental researches on these topics will be helpful to fulfill a complete picture on the spectra of the Pcsubscript𝑃𝑐Pc and Pc​ssubscript𝑃𝑐𝑠Pcs systems.

all_papers.title: Hyperon semileptonic decays in QCD sum rules
all_papers.abstract:
We investigate the hyperon semileptonic decays within the framework of QCD
sum rules. The flavor $ SU(3) $ symmetry breaking effects are analyzed via the
relevant form factors and corresponding branching fractions. Employing the $ z
$-series parameterization to capture the $ q^2 $ dependence of form factors, we
calculate the hyperon semileptonic decay rates and confront them with the
recent experimental measurements. Moreover, we calculate as well the
non-standard tensor form factors, which involve certain new physics beyond the
standard model.

all_papers.title: Anomalies in Hadronic $B$ Decays
all_papers.abstract:
In this paper, we perform fits to $B \to PP$ decays, where $B = \{B^0, B^+,
B_s^0\}$ and the pseudoscalar $P = \{\pi, K\}$, under the assumption of flavor
SU(3) symmetry [SU(3)$_F$]. Although the fits to $\Delta S=0$ or $\Delta S=1$
decays individually are good, the combined fit is very poor: there is a
$3.6\sigma$ disagreement with the SU(3)$_F$ limit of the standard model
(SM$_{\rm{SU(3)}_F}$). One can remove this discrepancy by adding
SU(3)$_F$-breaking effects, but 1000% SU(3)$_F$ breaking is required. The above
results are rigorous, group-theoretically - no dynamical assumptions have been
made. When one adds an assumption motivated by QCD factorization, the
discrepancy with the SM$_{\rm{SU(3)}_F}$ grows to $4.4\sigma$.

all_papers.title:
SU(3) symmetry analysis in charmed baryon two body decays with penguin
  diagram contribution
all_papers.abstract:
An increasing number of experimental measurements from the BESIII, Belle, and
Belle-II collaborations encourage investigations into charmed baryon two-body
decay processes. By including contributions from the penguin diagrams that are
ignored in previous studies, we perform a global analysis with SU(3) flavor
symmetry. Assuming all form factors are real, we achieve a remarkable minimal
$\chi^{2}/d.o.f = 0.788$ and find that the contribution of the amplitude
proportional to $V_{cb}^*V_{ub}$ is of the order $\sim 0.01$, comparable with
the contribution of the tree-level diagram. Additionally, by using the KPW
theorem to reduce the number of amplitudes from 13 to 7 in the leading
contribution, it becomes possible to consider the complex form factor case for
the leading IRA amplitude in the global analysis. However, the analysis of
complex form factors significantly conflicts with the experimental data
$Br(\Xi_c^0\to\Xi^-\pi^+)$, and by excluding this data, $\chi^2/d.o.f$ is
reduced from 5.95 to 1.19. Although the analysis of complex form factors shows
a significant central value of the penguin diagram contribution, the large
errors from the corresponding form factors make it a challenge to precisely
determine its true contribution. Consequently, the direct CP violation in decay
processes is predicted to be approximately zero. With more data in future
experiments, the penguin diagram contribution with the amplitude proportional
to $V_{cb}^*V_{ub}$ will be precisely determined, allowing for a more accurate
prediction of CP violation. Our analysis necessitates further theoretical
investigations and experimental measurements in the future.

all_papers.title:
Flavor-spin symmetry of the $P^N_{\psi}/H_{\Omega_{ccc}}^N$ and
  $P^\Lambda_{\psi s}/H^{\Lambda}_{\Omega_{ccc}s}$ molecular states
all_papers.abstract:
Based on a contact lagrangian that incorporates the SU(3) flavor and SU(2)
spin symmetries, we discuss the symmetry properties of the interactions among
the heavy flavor meson-baryon $P_{\psi}^N$, $P_{\psi s}^\Lambda$ (with quark
components [$n\bar{c}$][$nnc$], [$s\bar{c}$][$nnc$], or [$n\bar{c}$][$nsc$])
systems and di-baryon $H_{\Omega_{ccc}}^N$, $H^{\Lambda}_{\Omega_{ccc}s}$ (with
quark components [$nnc$][$ncc$], [$nnc$][$scc$] or [$nsc$][$ncc$]) systems
($n=u$, $d$). The light quark components of the $P_{\psi}^N$ ($P_{\psi
s}^\Lambda$) and $H_{\Omega_{ccc}}^N$ ($H_{\Omega_{ccc}s}^\Lambda$) systems
have identical flavors, the interactions generated from the exchanges of light
mesons in the $P_{\psi}^N$ ($P^\Lambda_{\psi s}$) systems should be very
similar to that of the $H_{\Omega_{ccc}}^N$ ($H^{\Lambda}_{\Omega_{ccc}s}$)
systems. We perform the single-channel and multi-channel calculations on the
$P_{\psi}^N/P^\Lambda_{\psi s}/H_{\Omega_{ccc}}^N/H^{\Lambda}_{\Omega_{ccc}s}$
systems and introduce the SU(3) breaking effect to identify the different mass
spectra among the $P_{\psi}^N$ ($H_{\Omega_{ccc}}^N$) and $P^\Lambda_{\psi s}$
($H^{\Lambda}_{\Omega_{ccc}s}$) systems. We suggest two kinds of evidences for
the existence of the flavor-spin symmetry among the heavy flavor
$P_{\psi}^N/H_{\Omega_{ccc}}^N/P^\Lambda_{\psi s}/H^{\Lambda}_{\Omega_{ccc}s}$
molecule community, i.e., the mass arrangements of the
$P_{\psi}^N/H_{\Omega_{ccc}}^N/P^\Lambda_{\psi s}/H^{\Lambda}_{\Omega_{ccc}s}$
mass spectra and the binding energies of the heavy flavor meson-baryon
(di-baryon) systems attributed to the same contact potentials.

all_papers.title: Chiral perturbation theory
all_papers.abstract:
In the limit of vanishing up, down and strange quark masses, QCD exhibits a
chiral symmetry. This symmetry is broken spontaneously to its vector subgroup,
giving rise to Goldstone bosons. These acquire a small mass through the
explicit chiral symmetry breaking for non-vanishing quark masses. The
consequences of these broken symmetries can be investigated in a suitably
tailored effective field theory called chiral pertubation theory. It admits a
perturbative expansion in the external momenta and the Goldstone boson masses
and can be systematically analyzed in terms of a loop expansion. The appearing
ultraviolet divergences in loop diagrams can be dealt with order-by-order
through the Goldstone boson contact interactions. Matter fields like the
lowest-lying baryons can also included, leading to a rich and testable
phenomenology of low-energy QCD.

all_papers.title: Hidden strangeness in meson weak decays to baryon pair
all_papers.abstract:
Our study focuses on the weak decay of $ D_s^+ \to p \overline{n} $, which is
the only possible two-body baryonic decay in the $ D $ meson system. An
analysis using perturbative quantum chromodynamics (pQCD) is challenging in
this decay due to the small amount of energy released. In particular, naive
factorization, suppressed by the light quark masses, results in a minor
contribution to this channel. In the framework of final state interactions, the
hidden strangeness in the intermediate state naturally avoids this chiral
suppression from light quark masses. The branching fraction is predicted to be
$ {\cal B}(D_s^+ \to p\overline{n}) = (1.43 \pm 0.10) \times 10^{-3} $, in
agreement with the experimental value of $ (1.22 \pm 0.11) \times 10^{-3} $. We
also analyze the decays of $ B $ mesons into two charmed baryons involving
annihilation-type topological diagrams. In these decays, we conduct a joint
analysis of naive factorization and final state interactions. Using the
experimental upper bound of $ {\cal B}(B_s^0 \to \Lambda_c^+
\overline{\Lambda}_c^-) < 8 \times 10^{-5} $, we set a constraint on the
coupling constant $ g_{D^+ \Lambda_c^+ n} < 7.5 $. Final state interactions
lead to a prediction of the decay parameter $ \gamma(B_s^0 \to \Lambda_c^+
\overline{\Lambda}_c^-) > 0.8 $, whereas pQCD predicts it to be negative. We
propose future measurements of $ B^0 \to \Xi_c^+ \overline{\Xi}_c^- $,
predicting a significant $ SU(3)_F $ breaking effect with $ \frac{{\cal B}(B^0
\to \Xi_c^+ \overline{\Xi}_c^-)}{{\cal B}(B_s^0 \to \Lambda_c^+
\overline{\Lambda}_c^-)} = 1.4\% $, contrary to the naive estimate of $ 5.3\%
$. We strongly recommend future measurements.

all_papers.title: Mass and decay width of $T_{ccs}$ from symmetries
all_papers.abstract:
We analyze the mass and width of the doubly heavy tetraquark $T_{ccs}$
composed of a heavy diquark and a light-quark cloud with strangeness with
assuming that a color antitriplet heavy diquark is a dominant component of the
doubly charmed tetraquarks $T_{cc}$ and $T_{ccs}$. We construct an effective
Lagrangian for masses of heavy hadrons based on the superflavor symmetry
between the doubly heavy tetraquarks and the singly heavy baryons by including
the terms that simultaneously break the heavy-quark and light-flavor
symmetries, and predict the mass of $T_{ccs}$ as $M(T_{ccs}) = 4047\pm11$\,MeV.
The comparison of this prediction with future experimental observation will
give a clue to understand the color structure of the heavy diquark. We also
predict the mass of $\Omega_{cc}$ as $M(\Omega_{cc}) = 3706^{+14}_{-15}\,$MeV.
We next calculate the decay width of $T_{ccs}$, based on solely the
light-flavor symmetry, as $\Gamma(T_{ccs}) = 42\pm 24$\,MeV.
